en: After an earlier world had been destroyed, the gods decided that people must live again. Quetzalcoatl, the Feathered Serpent, took on the task of finding the bones of the former race. He went down to Mictlan, the land of the dead, and stood before its lord Mictlantecuhtli. He asked for the bones so that new human beings could be made.

Mictlantecuhtli said Quetzalcoatl could have the bones if he walked around the underworld while sounding a shell trumpet. The shell had no holes, so Quetzalcoatl called on worms to pierce it and bees to make it sing. The ruler then allowed him to take the bones, but he secretly ordered a trap to be set in his path. Quetzalcoatl seized the bones and ran for the upper world.

As he fled, the ground opened and he fell into a pit. The bones dropped from his arms and broke into pieces of many sizes. Quetzalcoatl gathered them up and carried them to the place where the gods were waiting. He gave his own blood over the bones, and from them living people rose into the new world.
zh: 在更早的一个世界毁灭之后，众神决定要让人类再次出现。羽蛇神魁札尔科亚特尔承担了寻找旧人类骸骨的任务。他下到冥界米克特兰，站在冥界之主米克特兰特库特里面前。他请求取走那些骨头，好让新的世人被造出来。
米克特兰特库特里说，只要魁札尔科亚特尔能一边绕行冥界一边吹响海螺号角，就可以带走骨头。那只海螺本来没有孔洞，于是魁札尔科亚特尔叫来虫子钻孔，又让蜜蜂在里面发声。冥界之主表面上答应了他，却暗中命人沿路设下陷阱。魁札尔科亚特尔抱起骨头，向上界飞快逃去。
他逃走时，大地忽然裂开，他掉进了一个深坑。骨头从他怀里散落出来，摔成了大小不一的碎片。魁札尔科亚特尔把碎骨重新收起，带回众神等待的地方。他用自己的血洒在骨头上，于是活着的人类就在新的世界里诞生了。

en: When Vasilisa's mother was dying, she gave her daughter a small doll and told her to keep it always. After the mother died, Vasilisa's father married again. Her stepmother and stepsisters were harsh to her and loaded her with work. One night they put out every fire in the house and sent her into the forest to ask Baba Yaga for flame.

Deep in the woods, Vasilisa found Baba Yaga's hut standing on chicken legs in a clearing. She spoke to the hut, and it turned its door toward her. Baba Yaga arrived in her mortar, steering with a pestle and sweeping away her tracks with a broom. The old witch said Vasilisa could have fire only if she finished the housework and sorted the heaps of grain.

Vasilisa fed the little doll, and the doll helped her complete every task before morning. Baba Yaga saw that the work was done and asked how the girl had managed it. When Vasilisa said that her mother's blessing helped her, Baba Yaga shoved her away and gave her a skull lantern with burning eyes. Vasilisa carried it home, and its fire burned her stepmother and stepsisters to ashes, leaving her alive and free.
zh: 瓦西丽莎的母亲临终前，给了女儿一个小布偶，并叮嘱她一直带在身边。母亲死后，瓦西丽莎的父亲又娶了新妻子。继母和两个继姐对她十分刻薄，把重活都压在她身上。一天夜里，她们故意弄灭了家里的火，逼她到森林里向芭芭雅嘎去讨火种。
瓦西丽莎走进林中深处，看见空地上有一间长着鸡脚的小屋。她对小屋说了话，小屋便转过门来对着她。芭芭雅嘎坐着石臼飞来，用杵掌舵，再用扫帚抹去自己的踪迹。老巫婆说，只有瓦西丽莎替她做完家务并把成堆的谷物分拣好，才能带走火。
瓦西丽莎喂了小布偶，小布偶便在夜里帮她完成了一切活计。芭芭雅嘎看见工作都做完了，就问她是靠什么做到的。瓦西丽莎回答说，是母亲的祝福在帮助她，于是芭芭雅嘎立刻把她赶走，并给了她一盏长着燃烧眼睛的骷髅灯。瓦西丽莎提着骷髅灯回到家，灯里的火焰把继母和两个继姐烧成了灰，她自己则活了下来并得到自由。

en: Odysseus landed on an island with his men and found a cave full of food and pens for sheep and goats. He chose to wait inside to meet the owner instead of taking the supplies and leaving at once. The cave belonged to Polyphemus, a Cyclops who rolled a huge stone across the entrance when he returned. Odysseus and his men were trapped inside with the giant.

Polyphemus ignored their pleas and began seizing the men to eat them. Odysseus knew that killing the Cyclops outright would leave them sealed in the cave behind the great stone. He offered the giant strong wine and told him that his name was Nobody. When Polyphemus fell into a heavy sleep, Odysseus and his men drove a sharpened stake into his single eye and blinded him.

The Cyclops shouted for help, but when the others asked who was hurting him, he answered that Nobody was attacking him. At dawn Polyphemus opened the cave and felt the backs of his sheep as he let them out, hoping to catch the Greeks. Odysseus tied his men beneath the animals and clung under the largest ram, so they slipped outside unseen. Once he had reached the ship, he revealed his true name, and Polyphemus called on his father Poseidon to punish him on the long voyage home.
zh: 奥德修斯带着部下登上一座岛屿，发现了一处洞穴，里面堆着食物，还圈着羊和山羊。别人想拿了东西就走，奥德修斯却决定留下来，等洞穴的主人回来相见。那洞穴属于独眼巨人波吕斐摩斯，他回到洞里后用一块巨石堵住了洞口。奥德修斯和同伴就这样被困在了巨人里面。
波吕斐摩斯根本不理会他们的求饶，抓起奥德修斯的部下就生吃。奥德修斯知道如果直接杀死巨人，大家也无法搬开那块堵门的大石头。他先拿出烈酒给巨人喝，又骗他说自己的名字叫“无人”。等波吕斐摩斯醉倒熟睡后，奥德修斯和同伴把削尖的木桩刺进了他唯一的眼睛。
独眼巨人痛得大喊求救，别的独眼巨人赶来询问时，他却说是“无人”在害他。天亮后，波吕斐摩斯挪开石头放羊出去，一边摸着羊背，一边想把希腊人留在洞里。奥德修斯把同伴绑在羊腹下，自己也伏在最壮的一只公羊下面，于是众人悄悄逃了出去。等他回到船上后，他向巨人报出了自己的真名，波吕斐摩斯便向父亲波塞冬祈求，让奥德修斯在归途中受尽磨难。

en: When the army called for men to serve, Mulan saw that her aged father's name was on the draft rolls. He was too old and weak to endure another campaign, and her younger brother was still a child. Mulan decided that her family could not send him to war in that condition. She bought a horse and equipment and left home disguised as a man in her father's place.

Mulan joined the army and traveled with the troops over long distances through cold camps and dangerous marches. She kept her identity hidden while training, riding, and fighting beside the other soldiers. Year after year she served in the field and faced the same hardships as the men around her. Her skill and courage helped her survive until the war was won.

After victory, the ruler offered rewards and office to the returning soldiers. Mulan asked only for a swift horse so that she could go back to her home. When she put on her old clothes again and stepped out before her former comrades, they were astonished to see that she was a woman. They had fought beside her for years without knowing who she truly was.
zh: 朝廷征兵的时候，木兰看到军帖上写着自己父亲的名字。父亲年老体衰，已经难以再经受战场的劳苦，而弟弟又还年幼，无法代替他出征。木兰知道家里不能让父亲硬撑着上路。于是她买来马匹和装备，女扮男装代父从军。
木兰进入军中后，跟随大军长途跋涉，穿过荒野，也住过寒冷的营地。她始终把自己的身份隐藏起来，像其他士兵一样操练、骑马和作战。许多年里，她一直在军中服役，经历了和身边男子一样的艰苦。靠着本领和胆气，她一直活到战争结束。
凯旋之后，君主赏赐归来的将士，还想授给木兰官职。木兰没有接受这些封赏，只请求一匹快马，好让自己尽快回家。回到家中后，她换上旧时的衣裳，再次出现在旧日同袍面前。那些和她并肩作战多年的士兵这才惊讶地发现，她原来是个女子。

en: Aladdin was a poor young man when a sorcerer came to him pretending to be a relative. The sorcerer led him to a hidden cave and ordered him to bring back an old lamp from deep inside. Aladdin entered the cavern and found treasures there, but he refused to hand over the lamp before he was pulled out. The sorcerer sealed the cave in anger and left him trapped below the earth.

While searching for a way out, Aladdin used a magic ring and summoned a spirit that carried him home. Later he rubbed the dusty lamp and discovered that it held a far more powerful genie bound to serve its master. With the genie's help, Aladdin gained wealth, fine clothes, and a splendid palace. He used this new fortune to win the hand of the princess.

The sorcerer returned and tricked the princess into giving away the old lamp, then used the genie to carry the palace away with her inside. Aladdin was left with nothing but the magic ring and set out to find her. He reached the stolen palace, outwitted the sorcerer, and recovered the lamp. Once the lamp was back in his hands, the genie restored his power, and Aladdin destroyed his enemy and brought the princess home safely.
zh: 阿拉丁原本是个贫穷的年轻人，后来有个术士冒充亲戚来接近他。术士把他带到一处隐秘的地穴前，命他下去取回一盏旧灯。阿拉丁进入地下洞窟后看见了许多宝物，但他坚持要先被拉出去再交出神灯。术士恼怒之下封住洞口，把他独自困在了地下。
阿拉丁在绝境中摸索时，无意间用到了魔戒，召出了一个精灵，把他送回了家。后来他擦拭那盏旧灯，才发现灯中住着一个更强大的神灵，会服从灯主的命令。靠着神灯的力量，阿拉丁得到了财富、华服和宏伟的宫殿。凭借这一切，他终于娶到了公主。
术士后来又回来，骗公主用旧灯换新灯，把神灯重新骗到了自己手里，又命令灯神把整座宫殿连同公主一起搬走。阿拉丁转眼失去了一切，只能依靠魔戒去寻找公主的下落。他找到那座被夺走的宫殿后设法骗过术士，重新夺回了神灯。神灯回到手中后，阿拉丁再次命令灯神，除掉了敌人，并把公主平安带了回来。

en: Moses led the Israelites out of Egypt after Pharaoh finally allowed them to leave. Families set out together with their animals and possessions, moving away from the land where they had lived in bondage. Before long Pharaoh changed his mind and sent chariots and soldiers after them. The fleeing people soon found themselves trapped between the sea and the advancing army.

Fear spread through the camp as the Egyptians drew near behind them. Moses stood before the waters and stretched out his hand at God's command. A strong wind drove back the sea and opened a path across the seabed. The Israelites entered the passage and crossed with walls of water on both sides.

When the last of the Israelites had reached the far shore, the Egyptian forces rushed into the path after them. Moses stretched out his hand again, and the waters returned to their place. The sea closed over the chariots, the horses, and the soldiers of Pharaoh. The Israelites saw their pursuers destroyed and continued their journey into the wilderness.
zh: 法老终于答应放人后，摩西带领以色列人离开了埃及。各家各户带着家人、牲畜和随身财物一起上路，离开那片长期受奴役的土地。可是不久之后，法老又改变了主意，派出战车和军兵追赶他们。逃亡的人群很快就被逼到海边，前方是海水，后方是追兵。
看见埃及军队逼近，营中顿时充满了恐惧。摩西遵照上帝的命令，站在海边向海伸出手。强风整夜吹起，把海水向两边推开，在海中露出一条道路。以色列人便从这条路上走过去，两边都是高高立起的海水。
等最后一个以色列人到达对岸后，埃及的战车和士兵也冲进了海中的道路。摩西再次伸手，海水便回到了原处。汹涌的海浪淹没了法老的战车、马匹和军兵。以色列人看见追赶他们的人已经灭没，便继续向旷野前行。

en: When Achilles was an infant, his mother Thetis tried to make him invincible by dipping him into the river Styx. She held him by his heel, and the water made his body proof against weapons. The heel she gripped never touched the river and remained vulnerable. Thetis told no one about that single weak spot.

Achilles grew into the greatest warrior of the Greeks and went with them to fight at Troy. In the long siege he killed many Trojan heroes, including Hector before the city walls. Even the Trojans believed he could not be wounded in open combat. Apollo, who favored the Trojans, watched for the chance to bring him down.

Near the Scaean Gate, Paris drew his bow against Achilles. Guided by Apollo, the arrow struck the one place that could be pierced — the heel. Achilles fell and died of that wound, though no one had yet found the spot through fair fighting. Ever since, "Achilles' heel" has meant the single fatal weakness in a person who otherwise seems untouchable.
zh: 阿喀琉斯还是婴儿时，母亲忒提斯想让他刀枪不入。她抓着他的脚踵把他浸入冥河斯提克斯，河水让他全身都不再惧怕兵器。唯独她握住的那一处脚踵没有沾水，依旧是凡人之躯。忒提斯并未把这个弱点告诉任何人。
阿喀琉斯长大后成为希腊军中最善战的英雄，随大军前往特洛伊。在漫长的围城中，他斩杀了许多特洛伊勇士，连大将赫克托耳也死在他枪下。特洛伊人都相信，正面交锋谁也伤不了他。阿波罗站在特洛伊一方，一直在等机会要让他陨落。
在斯开亚城门附近，赫克托耳的弟弟帕里斯拉弓向阿喀琉斯射箭。阿波罗引导着这一箭，正中那唯一可破之处——他的脚踵。阿喀琉斯应声倒地，没有任何对手能在正面较量中找到这处弱点。此后人们便用「阿喀琉斯的脚踵」来比喻一个看似无敌之人身上唯一致命的弱处。

en: Medusa was once a beautiful young woman who served as a priestess. After she was violated in the temple of Athena, the goddess turned her into a monster. Her hair became living snakes, and her face became so terrible that anyone who looked directly at her turned to stone. From then on she lived with her two immortal Gorgon sisters at the edge of the world.

King Polydectes sent the young hero Perseus to fetch her head, expecting him to die in the attempt. The gods helped him: Hermes gave him winged sandals and Athena gave him a polished shield. Perseus found Medusa asleep and used the shield as a mirror so he could see her only by reflection. With one stroke he cut off her head.

From the wound sprang the winged horse Pegasus and the warrior Chrysaor. Perseus put the head into a bag and used it on his way home, turning enemies to stone with a single glimpse. At last he gave the head to Athena, who set it on her shield as a sign of terror in battle. Long after the body was gone, Medusa's gaze still answered any who challenged the goddess.
zh: 美杜莎本是一位美丽的年轻女子，曾在雅典娜神庙中担任女祭司。她在神庙里受到玷污后，雅典娜把怒火转到她身上，将她变成了怪物。她的头发化作群蛇，面容变得极为恐怖，凡是直接望见她的人都会立即化为石头。从那以后，她和两位不死的戈耳工姐妹隐居在世界尽头。
国王波吕得克忒斯派出年轻的英雄珀耳修斯去取她的头，本以为他必死无疑。诸神助他一臂之力：赫尔墨斯给他带翼凉鞋，雅典娜给他一面光亮如镜的盾牌。珀耳修斯找到正在沉睡的美杜莎，并未正面去看她。他借助盾牌当作镜子，只看反射出的影像，一剑便砍下了她的头。
从她颈中的伤口里跃出飞马珀伽索斯和勇士克律萨俄耳。珀耳修斯把头颅装进皮袋，在归途上用它对付仇敌，只一眼就能将他们变成石像。最后他把头颅献给雅典娜，女神将其安在自己的神盾上，作为战场上的恐怖标记。即便美杜莎的身体早已不在，那道目光仍在替女神威慑一切冒犯者。

en: Persephone, the daughter of Demeter, was gathering flowers when the earth opened beneath her. Hades, lord of the underworld, rose in his chariot and carried her down to his kingdom. He had asked Zeus for her as his wife, and Zeus had agreed without telling Demeter. Demeter searched the whole world for her daughter, refusing to let any plant grow until she was found.

As the harvest failed, mortals began to starve, and the gods on Olympus could receive no offerings. Zeus finally sent Hermes to bring Persephone back. Before she left, Hades offered her a pomegranate, and she ate a few of the seeds. By eating the food of the dead, she had bound herself to the underworld in part.

The gods reached a compromise: Persephone would spend part of each year above with her mother, and part below with Hades. When she returned to her mother, Demeter let the earth bloom, and that is summer; when she went back below, Demeter mourned and nothing grew, and that is winter. The seasons themselves were said to follow her movement between the two worlds.
zh: 得墨忒耳的女儿珀耳塞福涅正在草地上采花，脚下的大地忽然裂开。冥王哈得斯驾着马车冲了上来，把她带回了冥界。他事先向宙斯求娶她为妻，宙斯答应了，却没有告诉得墨忒耳。失去女儿后，得墨忒耳走遍人间寻找，并禁止地上的草木生长。
庄稼一片荒芜，凡人开始挨饿，奥林匹斯诸神也收不到祭品。宙斯只好派赫尔墨斯把珀耳塞福涅接回来。临走之前，哈得斯递给她一颗石榴，她尝了几粒籽。因为吃过冥界的食物，她就与冥界结下了割不断的联系。
众神最后定下折中之策：珀耳塞福涅每年要有一段时间回到母亲身边，又有一段时间回到哈得斯之处。她回到母亲身边时，得墨忒耳让大地开花结果，这便是夏天；她下到冥界时，得墨忒耳哀伤不已，草木凋零，这便是冬天。从此四季的更替，就被说成是她在两个世界之间往返的脚步。

en: King Minos of Crete kept a monster called the Minotaur, born to his wife Pasiphae and a divine bull. To hide the creature, the craftsman Daedalus built the Labyrinth beneath the palace, a maze from which no one could find a way out. Each year Athens had to send seven young men and seven young women to be devoured inside it. Prince Theseus offered himself among the next group of youths.

When he arrived in Crete, the king's daughter Ariadne fell in love with him and chose to help. She gave him a sword and a ball of thread, telling him to tie one end at the entrance and unwind it as he walked. Theseus entered the labyrinth, found the Minotaur in its inmost chamber, and killed it after a long struggle. Then he followed the thread back to the entrance.

Theseus sailed away with Ariadne and the surviving youths, but he abandoned her on the island of Naxos on the way home. He had agreed with his father, King Aegeus, to raise white sails if he came back alive, yet he forgot and left the black sails up. Aegeus saw the black sails from the cliffs and, believing his son dead, threw himself into the sea. From then on that sea was called the Aegean, after him.
zh: 克里特国王米诺斯养着一头怪物，叫做弥诺陶洛斯，是他的妻子帕西法厄和一头神牛所生。为了把怪物藏起来，工匠代达罗斯在王宫地下造了一座迷宫，进去就走不出来。每年雅典都要送来七名少年和七名少女，作为贡品被关进迷宫，让弥诺陶洛斯吞食。雅典王子忒修斯主动报名，混在下一批少年之中前往克里特。
他到达克里特后，国王的女儿阿里阿德涅爱上了他，决意暗中帮他。她给了他一把利剑和一团线，要他把线的一端系在迷宫入口，一边走一边放线。忒修斯进入迷宫，在最深处找到了弥诺陶洛斯，经过一番苦斗将其杀死。随后他循着那根线，原路找回入口。
忒修斯带着阿里阿德涅和幸存的雅典少年扬帆离开，归途上却把她抛在了那克索斯岛。他曾与父亲埃勾斯约定，如果平安归来便挂白帆，但他忘了换帆，黑色的帆仍旧高悬。埃勾斯在崖上看见那片黑帆，以为儿子已死，便跳海自尽。后世便把那片海称为爱琴海，以纪念他。

en: Jason was the rightful heir to the kingdom of Iolcus, but his uncle Pelias had seized the throne. To get rid of him, Pelias demanded that Jason fetch the Golden Fleece from distant Colchis. The fleece hung in a sacred grove guarded by a sleepless dragon. Jason had the ship Argo built and gathered a crew of heroes — the Argonauts.

After many adventures across the seas, the Argonauts reached Colchis, and Jason asked King Aeetes for the fleece. The king set him impossible trials: to yoke fire-breathing bulls, to sow dragon's teeth and fight the warriors that sprang from them, and to face the dragon itself. The king's daughter Medea, a sorceress, fell in love with Jason and gave him a salve that protected him from fire. With her help he survived every trial.

Medea led Jason to the grove and used her arts to put the dragon to sleep, and Jason seized the fleece. They fled aboard the Argo, pursued by her father, and at last reached Iolcus. There Jason claimed his throne, but his happiness with Medea did not last; he later abandoned her, and her revenge brought ruin on him. The Golden Fleece itself was hung in a temple as a sign of what the long voyage had cost.
zh: 伊阿宋本是伊俄尔科斯王国的正统继承人，王位却被叔父珀利阿斯篡夺。为了把他打发掉，珀利阿斯命他去遥远的科尔基斯取回金羊毛。那张金羊毛挂在一片圣林之中，由一条永不睡觉的恶龙守着。伊阿宋叫人造了一艘大船「阿尔戈号」，召集了一群英雄——阿尔戈英雄——同行。
在海上历经种种险境之后，阿尔戈英雄们终于来到科尔基斯，伊阿宋向国王埃厄忒斯求取金羊毛。国王给他设下种种几乎做不到的考验：要他驯服喷火的牛，要他播下龙牙、与从中跃出的战士厮杀，还要他面对那条恶龙本身。国王的女儿美狄亚是位女巫，爱上了伊阿宋，给他一种膏药，使他免受烈火所伤。靠她的帮助，他闯过了一道又一道难关。
美狄亚把伊阿宋带到圣林，用法术让恶龙陷入沉睡，他便顺势取下金羊毛。两人一起登上阿尔戈号逃亡，被她的父亲追在身后，最终回到伊俄尔科斯。在那里伊阿宋夺回了自己的王位，但他与美狄亚的姻缘并未善终；后来他抛弃了她，她的报复也让他一败涂地。那张金羊毛最后被供奉在神庙里，提醒人们这段远航付出的代价。

en: The Athenian craftsman Daedalus had been imprisoned with his son Icarus on the island of Crete by King Minos. To escape, Daedalus made two pairs of wings from feathers held together by wax. He warned Icarus not to fly too low, where sea spray would soak the wings, nor too high, where the sun would melt the wax. With this single warning he tied the wings onto the boy's shoulders.

The two of them rose into the air and flew away from Crete, passing over islands and open sea. Icarus, thrilled by the height, forgot his father's warning and began to climb higher and higher. The hot sun softened the wax that held the feathers together. One by one the feathers began to fall.

The wings came apart on his shoulders, and Icarus fell into the sea below. Daedalus could only watch from above as his son disappeared beneath the water. He flew on alone and reached safety in Sicily, where he buried his son. The sea where Icarus fell was thereafter called the Icarian Sea in memory of his fall.
zh: 雅典工匠代达罗斯和他的儿子伊卡洛斯被克里特国王米诺斯囚禁在岛上。为了脱身，代达罗斯用羽毛和蜡做了两副翅膀。他叮嘱伊卡洛斯，飞得太低不行，海浪会打湿翅膀；飞得太高也不行，太阳会把蜡晒化。他给儿子绑上翅膀时，只说了这一句叮咛。
父子俩升上天空，飞离克里特，越过一座座海岛和茫茫海面。伊卡洛斯被高空的兴奋冲昏了头，把父亲的告诫忘到脑后，越飞越高。烈日把粘住羽毛的蜡晒得软化，羽毛一根接一根地脱落下来。
翅膀在他肩上散了开来，伊卡洛斯坠入下方的大海。代达罗斯只能从高处眼睁睁地看着儿子消失在海面之下。他独自飞行，到达西西里岛后才得以安身，并把儿子安葬。从那以后，伊卡洛斯坠落的那片海，就被人们称作「伊卡里亚海」，以纪念他的陨落。

en: Sisyphus was the king of Corinth, famous for being clever and full of tricks. He cheated even Death itself: when Thanatos came to take him, he chained the god in his own house, and no mortal could die until the trick was found out. Later he persuaded Persephone to release him from the underworld so he could punish his wife, then refused to go back. The gods were determined to deal with him at last.

When Sisyphus finally died, the gods set a particular punishment for him in the underworld. He was made to roll a great boulder up a steep hill. Each time he came near the top, the stone slipped from his hands and rolled back to the bottom. He had to go down and push it up all over again.

There was never any end to the work, because the stone always rolled back at the last moment. Day after day Sisyphus pushed the same stone up the same hill, with no progress at all. The punishment was meant as the perfect answer to a man who had thought he could outwit death. Ever since, a "Sisyphean task" has meant a piece of work that never finishes no matter how hard one struggles.
zh: 西西弗斯是科林斯的国王，以机智多谋而出名。他甚至欺骗过死神：塔那托斯前来取他的命时，他反把死神锁在自己家中，结果一时之间凡人都不能再死，直到这把戏被识破。后来他又骗冥后珀耳塞福涅放他回阳间惩罚妻子，回来之后却拒不再下冥界。诸神决定要彻底处置他。
西西弗斯死后，诸神在冥界给他安排了一个特别的刑罚。他必须把一块巨石推上陡峭的山顶。每当他快推到顶时，石头都会从他手中滑脱，重新滚回山脚。他只能再下去，从头把它推上来。
这件苦工永远完不了，因为巨石总在最后关头滚落。日复一日，西西弗斯推着同一块石头爬同一座山，毫无进展。这场刑罚正是为那个自以为能戏弄死亡的人精心准备的回应。从此以后，「西西弗斯的苦工」就用来形容那种无论如何努力也永远做不完的事。

en: Heracles was the son of Zeus and the mortal Alcmene. Hera hated him from birth and later sent a fit of madness on him, in which he killed his own wife and children. To atone, he was sent to serve his cousin King Eurystheus and perform whatever tasks the king set. Eurystheus, who feared and disliked him, set him twelve labors of impossible difficulty.

The labors took him to the ends of the known world. He strangled the Nemean lion whose hide was proof against weapons, killed the many-headed Hydra of Lerna, captured the Erymanthian boar and the Ceryneian hind, cleaned the immense stables of Augeas in a single day, drove off the Stymphalian birds, and brought back the Cretan bull. He fetched the man-eating mares of Diomedes and the belt of Hippolyta, queen of the Amazons.

In the last labors he traveled even farther, taking the cattle of the giant Geryon at the western edge of the earth, picking the golden apples of the Hesperides, and finally going down to the underworld to bring back the three-headed dog Cerberus alive. After completing all twelve, Heracles was freed from Eurystheus' service. The labors made his name and were remembered everywhere he had passed.
zh: 赫拉克勒斯是宙斯与凡间女子阿尔克墨涅之子。赫拉自他出生起就恨他，后来又让他陷入疯狂，使他亲手杀死了自己的妻儿。为了赎罪，他被派去侍奉表兄欧律斯透斯王，让国王差遣他做事。欧律斯透斯既怕他又妒他，便给他出了十二道几乎不可能完成的难题。
这些苦差把他带到了已知世界的各个角落。他空手扼死了刀枪不入的涅墨亚狮子，斩杀了勒拿沼地的九头蛇许德拉，活捉了厄律曼托斯野猪与刻律涅亚牝鹿，一天之内清理了奥革阿斯庞大的牲畜栏，赶走了斯廷法利斯湖的怪鸟，又制服了克里特公牛。他还夺来了狄俄墨得斯的食人马群，以及阿玛宗女王希波吕忒的腰带。
在最后几桩苦差中，他走得更远——西到大地尽头夺取巨人革律翁的牛群，去赫斯佩里得斯的园中摘下金苹果，最后又下到冥界，把三头犬刻耳柏洛斯活活带回阳间。十二桩苦差全部完成之后，赫拉克勒斯就摆脱了为欧律斯透斯效劳的义务。这些功绩让他的名字传遍各地，每一处他到过的地方都留下了关于他的传说。

en: Orpheus was the greatest musician among the Greeks; his lyre could move stones, animals, and even the gods. He loved his bride Eurydice, but on the day of their wedding she stepped on a snake and died. Unable to accept the loss, Orpheus went down into the underworld to find her. There he played his music before Hades and Persephone.

The music was so beautiful that the rulers of the dead were moved to tears. They agreed to let Eurydice return with him to the world above. They set only one condition: Orpheus must walk in front of her on the way out, and he must not look back at her until they had both reached the daylight. He gave his promise and began the long climb.

Near the end of the path, Orpheus could no longer bear not to see her, and he turned to look. Eurydice was just behind him, but the moment his eyes met hers she was pulled back into the underworld, this time forever. Orpheus tried to follow, but the gates were closed against him. He returned to the world above alone, and from then on his songs were full of grief.
zh: 俄耳甫斯是希腊最伟大的乐者，他的里拉琴声能感动石头、动物，乃至诸神。他深爱新婚的妻子欧律狄刻，可就在婚礼当天，她被毒蛇咬到，当即身亡。俄耳甫斯不肯认命，独自下到冥界去寻她。他在哈得斯与珀耳塞福涅面前奏起琴声。
那琴声美得让冥界之主和冥后都为之动容。他们答应让欧律狄刻随他回到阳世，只立下一个条件：归途上俄耳甫斯必须走在前面，不许回头看她，直到两人都重新走到日光下为止。他答应下来，便开始了漫长的攀登。
走到接近出口时，俄耳甫斯再也忍不住要看一眼妻子，便回过头去。欧律狄刻就在他身后，可眼神相接的那一刻，她被一股力量重新拽回冥界，从此不再返回。俄耳甫斯想跟下去，冥府的大门却已对他紧闭。他只身回到人间，从那以后他的歌声中只剩下哀伤。

en: Romulus and Remus were twin brothers, sons of the priestess Rhea Silvia and the god Mars. Their great-uncle, who had seized the throne of Alba Longa, ordered the babies to be drowned in the Tiber. The basket holding them was caught in the reeds at the riverbank. A she-wolf found them there and suckled them, and a shepherd later took them in and raised them.

When the brothers grew up they discovered their origin, killed their great-uncle, and restored their grandfather to the throne. They then decided to found a city of their own near the river where they had been saved. Each chose a different hill, and they quarreled over which hill to build on and over who would give the new city its name. Romulus began to mark out walls on the Palatine.

Remus mocked the new walls by leaping across them, and in the quarrel that followed Romulus killed his brother. The city he built then bore his name — Rome. Romulus filled it with outcasts and runaways and made himself its first king. From that single founding act, Rome traced its long history back to a wolf-suckled twin who had killed his own brother.
zh: 罗穆路斯和雷穆斯是一对孪生兄弟，是女祭司雷亚·西尔维娅与战神玛尔斯之子。他们的舅祖父篡夺了阿尔巴朗加的王位，下令将两个婴儿丢进台伯河里淹死。盛着他们的篮子被搁浅在岸边的芦苇丛中。一头母狼发现了他们，用乳汁哺育了他们，后来又被一位牧人收养长大。
兄弟二人长大后查清了身世，杀掉舅祖父，把外祖父重新扶上王位。随后他们决定，在当年自己得救的那条河边自己建一座城。两人各看中一座山头，因为该在哪里筑墙、新城又该用谁的名字，争执不下。罗穆路斯开始在帕拉蒂尼山上划定城墙。
雷穆斯不屑地跃过那刚画好的墙界以示嘲弄，争执之中罗穆路斯怒而杀死了亲弟。后来他所建的这座城便以他的名字命名——罗马。他广纳逃亡者和流民，自任为第一位国王。罗马漫长的历史，便是从这一桩由狼乳哺育、又亲手弑兄的开端讲起。

en: King Midas of Phrygia did a kindness to Silenus, an old companion of the god Dionysus, when Silenus was found wandering lost in his realm. To thank him, Dionysus offered Midas anything he wished. Midas asked that everything he touched might turn to gold. The god, with some misgiving, granted the wish.

At first Midas was delighted. He touched a twig, and it became gold; he touched a stone, and it became gold. But when he sat down to eat, the bread turned to gold in his hands, and the wine turned to gold on his lips. When his daughter ran to embrace him, she too became a lifeless golden statue under his touch.

Horrified, Midas begged Dionysus to take back the gift. The god told him to wash himself in the river Pactolus, and the gold-touching power went out of him into the river. From that day, fine gold was said to be found in the sands of the Pactolus. Midas himself never again loved gold the way he had before.
zh: 弗里吉亚国王迈达斯有一回收留了狄俄倪索斯的老伴侣西勒诺斯，那位老人是在他领地里迷了路被人发现的。狄俄倪索斯为感谢他，许他一个愿望。迈达斯求道：愿自己所触之物都化作黄金。狄俄倪索斯心有些迟疑，却还是答应了。
起初迈达斯欣喜若狂。他碰一下树枝，树枝变成金枝；他碰一下石头，石头变成金块。然而当他坐下吃饭，面包一入手便化作黄金，酒一沾唇也凝成黄金。他的女儿欢喜地扑过来抱他，刚一接触，也立刻变成了一尊冰冷的金像。
惊恐之下，迈达斯哀求狄俄倪索斯收回这份本领。神让他到帕克托罗斯河中洗浴，他身上「点金」的力量便随水流走，留在了河里。从那以后人们便说，那条河的沙中能淘到细金。迈达斯本人则再也不像从前那样痴迷黄金了。

en: After ten years of besieging Troy, the Greeks could not break through the city walls. Odysseus proposed a trick: they would build a huge wooden horse, hide a small body of soldiers inside it, and leave it on the shore as if it were an offering. The rest of the army would pretend to sail away and hide behind a nearby island. The Trojans would, they hoped, drag the horse into the city themselves.

The horse was built and left on the empty beach. The Trojans came out, found it, and argued about what to do. Some, including the priest Laocoon, warned them to destroy it. But a Greek named Sinon, left behind on purpose, told them a careful lie about why the horse should be brought inside as a gift to Athena. The Trojans pulled the horse through the gates and celebrated victory.

That night, while the city slept and the Greek soldiers in the horse waited, Sinon opened a small door in the horse's belly. The hidden warriors climbed out and opened the gates of Troy to the returning army. The city was sacked and burned, the men killed, and the women taken captive. The wooden horse became the lasting image of how a long war was decided by a single deception.
zh: 希腊人围困特洛伊整整十年，却始终攻不破城墙。奥德修斯献出一计：造一匹巨大的木马，把一小队精兵藏在里面，留在岸边，伪装成留给神明的祭礼。其余大军则假装拔锚扬帆，实际上躲到附近一座小岛后面。他们盘算着特洛伊人会自己把木马拖进城里。
木马造好后被丢在空旷的海滩上。特洛伊人出城察看，对怎么处置这匹木马争执不下。其中祭司拉奥孔等人极力主张毁掉它。但希腊人故意留下的西农讲了一套精心编好的谎话，让特洛伊人相信这是献给雅典娜的礼物，应当抬进城里。特洛伊人于是把木马拖入城门，开宴庆祝胜利。
当夜全城酣睡，木马腹中的希腊兵静静等候，西农悄悄打开木马腹下的小门。藏身其中的将士爬出来，又把特洛伊城门为已经返回的大军打开。整座城被焚毁洗劫，男子尽遭斩杀，妇孺沦为俘虏。从此那匹木马便成了一段千古印象——一场十年的围城，最后是被一记骗局决定胜负。

en: Arachne was a young woman from Lydia whose skill at weaving had become famous through all the country. Travelers came just to watch her hands and her loom. She grew so proud of her work that she claimed she could weave better than Athena, the goddess of weaving herself. When Athena heard the boast, she came in disguise as an old woman and warned the girl to honor the gods. Arachne answered with contempt, and Athena revealed herself.

The goddess accepted the challenge. The two women set up their looms side by side and began to weave. Athena wove the gods of Olympus in their majesty, and around the edges she showed mortals who had been punished for daring to compare themselves with the gods. Arachne wove scenes in which the gods had behaved badly toward mortals — seductions, betrayals, deceptions. Her work was technically perfect, and the truth in it stung.

Furious, Athena tore apart Arachne's tapestry and struck her with the shuttle. Arachne, ashamed and broken, tried to hang herself. Athena would not let her die quite that way; she turned her into a spider, condemned to keep on spinning forever from her own body. From that day onward, spiders have woven their fine threads everywhere as her descendants.
zh: 阿拉克涅是吕底亚地方的少女，纺织技艺名满全国。旅人专程前来只为看她的双手和织机。她对自己的手艺日益自负，竟扬言自己织得比纺织女神雅典娜还好。雅典娜听到此言，化作老妪前来，劝她敬重神明。阿拉克涅口出狂言加以蔑视，雅典娜便现出真身。
女神接下她的挑战。两人面对面架起织机一起动手。雅典娜织出奥林匹斯诸神威严的姿态，周围还点缀了那些因敢于与神比较而受罚的凡人。阿拉克涅则织出诸神种种对凡间为非作歹的丑事——勾引、背叛、欺骗。她的手艺无懈可击，画面的讽刺意味也刺人心痛。
雅典娜怒火中烧，撕碎了阿拉克涅的织毯，又用织梭打在她头上。羞愤之下，阿拉克涅想自缢以了断。雅典娜不肯让她这样死去，便把她变成了一只蜘蛛，终生只能从自己的身体里抽丝结网。自那以后，蜘蛛便作为她的后代，遍处吐着细细的丝线。

en: The hammer Mjolnir was forged for Thor by two dwarves, Brokkr and Sindri, as part of a wager with Loki. Loki distracted one of them at the forge by changing into a biting fly, and the handle came out shorter than intended. Even so, the finished hammer never missed its mark when thrown, always returned to Thor's hand, and could be made small enough to hide. Thor wore iron gloves to grip it and a belt that doubled his strength.

One day the giant Thrym stole the hammer and hid it deep in the earth, demanding the goddess Freyja as ransom. The gods refused to give her up. Loki proposed instead that Thor go disguised as Freyja, wearing the bride's veil and dress. Thor agreed, with great reluctance, and went into the giant's hall as the supposed bride.

At the wedding feast Thrym brought out Mjolnir to bless the bride, placing it for a moment on Thor's lap. Thor seized the hammer and laid about him on every side, killing Thrym and all the giants in the hall. He returned with Mjolnir to Asgard, and after that the hammer stood as the chief defense of gods and men against the powers of giant-kind.
zh: 雷神之锤姆乔尔尼尔是两位侏儒布罗克与辛德里为托尔锻造的，那是他们与洛基打赌的一部分。洛基化成飞蝇骚扰其中一人，结果锤柄被打得比原计划略短。即便如此，这把锤子掷出之后总能精准命中目标，又自动回到托尔手中，还可以缩小到便于收藏。托尔要戴上一双铁手套握它，再系上一条加倍力量的腰带。
有一天，巨人特里姆偷走了这把锤子，将它深深地藏在地下，要诸神交出女神弗蕾娅作为换回的代价。诸神不肯把她交出去。洛基便提议让托尔扮成弗蕾娅，披上新娘的面纱和长裙。托尔万般不情愿，最后还是同意了，以「新娘」的身份走进了巨人的厅堂。
婚宴上，特里姆取出姆乔尔尼尔为新娘祝福，把锤子暂放在「她」膝上。就在这一刻，托尔一把抓起锤子，向四面八方挥砍开来，杀掉了特里姆和厅中所有的巨人。他带着姆乔尔尼尔回到阿斯加德，从此这把锤子便成了诸神和人类抵御巨人族的主要倚仗。

en: The dragon Fafnir had once been a dwarf, but greed for a cursed golden hoard had transformed him. He coiled around the treasure on a wide heath, and no one dared come near. The smith Regin, Fafnir's own brother, longed for the gold and fostered the young hero Sigurd to win it for him. Regin forged Sigurd a sword called Gram from the broken shards of his father's blade.

Regin advised Sigurd to dig a pit on the path Fafnir used to crawl down to the water, and to strike upward from below. Sigurd dug the pit and waited inside it. When Fafnir came down to drink, the hero thrust Gram up through the soft underside, and the dragon died in great agony. Before he died, Fafnir warned Sigurd that the hoard was cursed.

Sigurd then roasted Fafnir's heart over a fire for Regin. He touched it with his finger to test if it was done, burned himself, and put his thumb in his mouth — and the moment dragon's blood touched his lips, he could understand the speech of birds. The birds in the trees warned him that Regin meant to kill him next. Sigurd killed Regin instead and rode away with the cursed gold, but the curse soon caught up with him.
zh: 恶龙法夫纳本是矮人族出身，因贪求那堆被诅咒的金宝，化成了巨龙。他盘踞在一片荒原上的金堆之上，无人敢靠近。法夫纳的兄弟、铁匠雷金一直觊觎那批宝藏，便收养年轻的英雄西古尔德为养子，要他替自己去取金子。雷金用西古尔德父亲遗留的断剑碎片，为他重新锻造了一柄叫做格拉姆的宝剑。
雷金劝西古尔德在法夫纳爬下来饮水的小路上挖一个深坑，等他从上面爬过时自下而上一击。西古尔德便挖好坑，藏身其中守候。法夫纳下来饮水时，他用格拉姆从下方扎入恶龙柔软的腹下，恶龙在剧痛中死去。临终前，法夫纳警告西古尔德这批金宝带着诅咒。
随后西古尔德按雷金吩咐烤龙心。他用手指试一试是否熟透，烫到了自己，便把拇指放进嘴里——龙血一沾到舌头，他立刻听懂了鸟语。树上的群鸟告诉他，雷金接下来打算杀掉他。西古尔德先下手为强，杀了雷金，带着那堆受诅咒的黄金离开。但那一份诅咒，没多久也跟上了他。

en: Baldr, son of Odin and Frigg, was the most beloved of all the Norse gods. After he began having dreams of his own death, his mother Frigg traveled through the worlds and asked every kind of thing — metals, stones, plants, animals — to swear an oath never to harm him. Every kind of thing swore the oath, and afterwards the gods amused themselves by throwing weapons at Baldr to see them fall away without effect. Only one small plant, the mistletoe, Frigg had not bothered to ask, thinking it too young and weak to matter.

Loki discovered this oversight. He shaped a dart from the mistletoe and brought it to the blind god Hodr, who was standing at the edge of the game, unable to join in. Loki guided Hodr's hand, telling him to honor his brother by throwing the dart. Hodr threw it, the mistletoe pierced Baldr, and he fell down dead.

The gods mourned, and Hermod rode down to the underworld to ask the goddess Hel to release him. Hel agreed on the condition that every creature in the world weep for him. Messengers went everywhere, and all things wept — except for one giantess, who is thought to have been Loki in disguise. Because of that single refusal, Baldr remained in the underworld, and his death was kept as a sign that Ragnarok was drawing near.
zh: 巴德尔是奥丁与弗丽嘉之子，在北欧诸神中最得众神喜爱。他开始梦见自己的死亡之后，母亲弗丽嘉走遍诸世，让金属、石头、植物、动物等等一切事物都立下誓言，永远不会伤害巴德尔。一切事物都立下了誓言，从此诸神便把投掷武器到巴德尔身上当作消遣，看着它们一一从他身上无害地落下。只有一种小小的植物——槲寄生，弗丽嘉没有费心去问，觉得它太年轻太柔弱，不值一提。
洛基探知了这一漏洞。他用槲寄生做了一支飞镖，递给站在游戏边上、因为眼盲无法参与的霍德尔。洛基扶着他的手，告诉他这样可以表达对兄长的敬意，让他投出去。霍德尔依言一掷，槲寄生穿透了巴德尔的身体，巴德尔当场身亡。
诸神哀痛不已，赫尔莫德骑马下到冥界，请求冥后赫尔放巴德尔回去。赫尔提出条件：除非世上一切都为他哭泣，才可放人。使者四处奔走，万物果然都为他哭泣——只有一位女巨人不肯，那人据说正是洛基化身的。就因为这一个不肯，巴德尔仍要留在冥界，他的死也被视为「诸神黄昏」临近的征兆。

en: After Baldr's death the gods learned that Loki had been the one who guided Hodr's hand. Loki fled and tried to hide by changing into a salmon and leaping in a waterfall. The gods came to the falls, threw a great net into the water, and finally caught him as he tried to leap clear. They brought him back to a cave to be punished.

The gods stretched Loki across three sharp stones, binding him with the entrails of his own son, which had been turned to iron. Above his face the goddess Skadi hung a venomous snake, so that its poison would drip onto him drop by drop. Loki's wife Sigyn sat beside him with a bowl to catch the venom before it could fall on his face. So long as she held the bowl steady, he was spared.

But every time she had to turn aside to empty the bowl, drops of venom struck his face, and Loki's whole body would writhe in pain. The shaking, the old stories say, is the cause of earthquakes. Loki must lie in those bonds until the end of the world, when Ragnarok comes. Then he will break free at last and lead the enemies of the gods into the final battle.
zh: 巴德尔死后，诸神查出当初牵着霍德尔的手的人正是洛基。洛基逃走躲藏，变成一条鲑鱼潜入瀑布之中。诸神追到瀑布前，把一张大网撒入水里，趁他跃身腾空时终于将他擒获。他们把他带回一个山洞里行刑。
诸神把洛基横放在三块尖石上，用他亲生儿子化作铁条的肠子把他捆住。女神斯卡蒂在他脸前悬挂了一条毒蛇，让毒液一滴一滴地往下渗。洛基的妻子西吉恩坐在他身旁，托着一只钵接住毒液，不让它溅到他脸上。只要她稳稳地端着钵，洛基便能勉强忍受。
然而每当她不得不转身倾倒钵中毒液时，毒液就会落到洛基脸上，他全身都会因剧痛而剧烈颤抖。古老传说便说，地震正是由他这阵翻滚引起的。洛基必须以这副捆绑之姿躺到世界末日为止，等到诸神黄昏来临的那一天。届时他才会终于挣脱开来，率领诸神的敌人投入那场最后的战斗。

en: In Egyptian belief, the sun god Ra sailed across the sky every day in his solar boat, bringing light to the world. When evening came he descended below the western horizon and entered the underworld, the Duat. There the boat had to travel through the twelve hours of the night, each hour with its own gate and its own dangers. The souls of the dead and the gods of the underworld waited for him along the way.

The greatest danger of every night was the serpent Apophis, who lurked in the river of the underworld and tried each night to swallow the boat and stop the sun from rising. The god Seth stood in the boat's prow with a spear, ready to strike the serpent down. Other gods joined Ra at different hours of the night to renew his strength. At the deepest hour Ra met his own dead body and exchanged power with it.

Each morning the boat reached the eastern horizon, and Ra rose again as the sun. The cycle was understood to repeat without end, but it was never automatic; it had to be won every single night. Funerary texts walked the souls of the dead through the same twelve hours, so that they too could rise with Ra at dawn. The journey of the sun beneath the world stood at the center of Egyptian thinking about death and rebirth.
zh: 在古埃及的信仰中，太阳神拉每天驾着太阳船在天上巡行，把光带给世界。傍晚他降到西方地平线之下，进入冥府杜阿特。在那里太阳船要穿过黑夜十二个时辰，每一时辰各有一道门户和各自的危险。亡魂与各路冥神都在路边等候他的到来。
每个夜晚最大的威胁是巨蛇阿波菲斯。它潜伏在冥河之中，每夜都想吞下太阳船，让太阳无法重新升起。塞特神立在船首举矛，准备击杀这条蛇。其他诸神在不同的时辰陆续登船，为拉补充力量。在最深的那一时辰，拉与自己已死的身体相遇，两者交换力量。
黎明时分太阳船到达东方地平线，拉以太阳的形象再度升起。这场轮回被理解为永不止息，却也并不会自然而然——每一晚都必须重新赢得一次。陪葬文献便陪着亡灵走过这十二个时辰，好让他们也能随拉一同在朝阳中重生。太阳在世界之下的这段夜行，是古埃及思考死亡与重生的核心意象。

en: The goddess Inanna, queen of heaven and earth, decided to descend to the underworld, ruled by her sister Ereshkigal. She put on her seven divine ornaments and left orders with her servant Ninshubur to call on the gods for help if she did not return. At the first gate of the underworld she was stopped, and the gatekeeper said she must obey the laws of that place. As she passed through each of the seven gates, one of her ornaments was taken from her.

By the time she stood before Ereshkigal's throne she was naked and powerless. Ereshkigal's judges fixed her with the eye of death, and she was struck down and hung on a hook on the wall like a piece of meat. Three days and three nights passed, and she did not return. Ninshubur went from god to god begging help, and finally the wise Enki agreed to act.

Enki made two small creatures from the dirt under his fingernails and sent them down with the food and water of life. They slipped past the gates, found Inanna's body, and restored her. The judges allowed her to leave only on the condition that someone be sent below in her place. When she returned and found her husband Dumuzi seated comfortably on her throne, she chose him; thereafter Dumuzi spent half of each year in the underworld, and his return to the surface became a season of growth.
zh: 天与地的女主伊南娜决定下到由她姐姐埃列什基伽尔统治的冥界。她戴上自己的七件神物准备启程，并嘱咐侍女宁舒布尔：若她久去不归，就请众神出面相救。在冥界第一道大门前她被拦下，门神告诉她，到了这里就要守这里的规矩。她每过一道门，便被取走一件神物，七道门走完，她已一无所有。
走到埃列什基伽尔王座前时，她已赤身无力。冥界的判官以「死亡之眼」看向她，她当即倒下，被钉在墙上的钩子上，像挂着一块肉。三天三夜过去，她始终没有回来。宁舒布尔挨家挨户去求众神出手，最后智慧之神恩基答应相助。
恩基从指甲里搓出两只小造物，让它们带着「生命之食」和「生命之水」下到冥界。它们穿过道道大门，找到伊南娜的身体并将她救活。冥界判官放她离开，条件是必须有人替她下来。她回到上界，发现丈夫杜穆兹仍安然坐在她的王座上，便选了他做替身；从此杜穆兹每年要在冥界待上半年，他每次重返人间的时节，也成了万物生长的季节。

en: The archer Hou Yi was rewarded with an elixir of immortality after he shot down nine of the ten suns that had been scorching the earth. He brought the elixir home and stored it carefully, intending to share it one day with his beloved wife Chang'e so they could become immortals together. One night, while Hou Yi was away, an apprentice tried to seize the elixir by force. To keep it from falling into the wrong hands, Chang'e drank it herself.

Her body grew light, and she could no longer stay on the earth. She rose slowly into the sky, drifting higher and higher, until she reached the moon. There she stayed, far from her husband and the human world. The cold palace of the moon became her dwelling.

When Hou Yi came home and learned what had happened, he gazed up at the round moon and set out her favorite foods as an offering. People copied him, looking up at the autumn moon and laying out cakes and fruit in remembrance of her loneliness. So began the Mid-Autumn Festival, when families gather under the same bright moon and remember Chang'e in her distant palace.
zh: 神射手后羿射下了九个炙烤大地的太阳，得到了一颗不死灵药作为赏赐。他把灵药带回家中小心收好，打算有朝一日与心爱的妻子嫦娥同服，一起飞升成仙。某天后羿外出，一个徒弟见他不在，便强逼嫦娥交出灵药。为了不让灵药落入坏人之手，嫦娥情急之下自己把它吞下。
她的身体变得轻飘飘，再也留不住在大地上。她缓缓飘升入天，越升越高，最终抵达月亮。从此她留在月宫，远离丈夫与人间。寒冷的月宫成了她栖身的地方。
后羿回家后听说原委，仰望那轮圆月，把妻子平日喜爱的食物摆出来祭她。世人也仿效他的做法，在秋夜望月时摆上月饼瓜果，思念远在月宫的嫦娥。中秋节就这样流传下来——人们在同一轮明月下团聚，也想起那位独居月宫的女子。

en: After Sun Wukong learned the seventy-two transformations and obtained the iron staff from the Dragon King's palace, the Jade Emperor tried to keep him under control by giving him minor posts in heaven. One of these was to look after the Peach Garden of immortality, whose peaches ripened once in many thousand years and granted long life. Left alone among the trees, Sun Wukong helped himself to the ripe peaches whenever he liked.

When the Queen Mother of the West held her grand Peach Banquet, she did not invite him. Furious at the slight, Sun Wukong stole into the banquet hall, ate the prepared food, drank the immortal wine, and then went on to Laozi's furnace and swallowed the elixir pills. Drunk and free of consequence, he returned to his mountain and declared himself "Great Sage Equal to Heaven." Soldiers were sent against him, but no celestial army could subdue him.

At last the Buddha himself was called. He proposed a wager: if Sun Wukong could jump out of the Buddha's palm, heaven would yield to him. Sun Wukong leapt across what he thought was the entire universe, marked a pillar at the world's edge, and returned, only to find he had never left the Buddha's hand. The Buddha closed his palm into a mountain and pinned Sun Wukong beneath it, where he waited five hundred years for the pilgrim Tang Sanzang to come.
zh: 孙悟空学得七十二变、从龙宫得到金箍棒之后，玉皇大帝想用些小官职把他笼络住。其中一项差事，就是看管那座蟠桃园——园中桃子要千万年才熟一回，吃了能长生不老。守园人不在身旁，孙悟空便天天偷吃熟透的仙桃。
王母娘娘举办盛大的蟠桃会，却没请孙悟空。他被怠慢得火起，闯进席上，先把宴中珍馐吃个精光，再把仙酒一并喝尽，又顺势跑到太上老君的丹炉前，把炼好的金丹也吞了下去。带着醉意逃回花果山，他自封为「齐天大圣」。玉帝派出天兵讨伐，却没有一支天兵能压制他。
最后只好请来如来佛祖。佛祖与他打赌：若他能跳出如来手掌心，天庭便让位给他。孙悟空一翻身跳出，自以为已飞到宇宙尽头，便在「最远的柱子」上做了记号，回来时却发现自己始终未曾离开如来的手掌。如来一翻掌化作五行山，把孙悟空压在山下，他便在那里等了五百年，直到取经人唐三藏路过此地。

en: The fisherman Urashima Taro one day rescued a small turtle that children were tormenting on the shore. Later, while he was fishing, a great turtle rose from the sea and thanked him for saving its child. It offered to carry him to the Dragon Palace beneath the waves. Urashima climbed onto its back and was taken down to a shining palace at the bottom of the sea.

There the princess Otohime welcomed him with feasts, dances, and the company of beautiful sea creatures. Day after day passed in joy, and Urashima did not notice how the time slipped by. After what felt like only a few days, he began to long for his home and his old mother. Otohime begged him to stay, but at last she let him go, giving him a small lacquered box and warning him never to open it.

The turtle carried him back to the shore he remembered. But the village had changed: he knew no one, his home was gone, and people spoke of an Urashima Taro who had disappeared at sea long ago. In confusion and grief he opened the box, hoping for some help. A white cloud rose out of it, and in an instant he became old, his beard turned white, and he fell down on the sand — the years he had not lived under the sea had finally found him.
zh: 渔夫浦岛太郎有一天在海边救下了一只被孩童捉弄的小海龟。后来他出海打鱼，一只大海龟从浪中浮出，向他道谢，说自己便是当日小龟的亲族。它请浦岛太郎到海底龙宫去做客。浦岛太郎骑上龟背，被带到了海底一座光芒四溢的宫殿。
宫中乙姬公主以盛宴、歌舞和海中各色生灵殷勤款待他。一天又一天过得欢乐无比，浦岛太郎竟没察觉时间飞逝。他自以为只过了几日，心中却开始挂念家乡和年迈的母亲。乙姬苦苦相留，最终还是答应放他回去，临别时送他一只小漆盒，叮嘱他千万不要打开。
海龟把他送回他熟悉的海岸，可村中早已物是人非：他一个人也不认识，故居已不复存在，村人只听过一个「很久以前出海未归的浦岛太郎」。在惶惑悲伤中，他打开了那只小漆盒，想从中找一点指引。盒中升起一缕白烟，瞬间他须发皆白，身躯衰老，倒在了沙地上——他在海底没有度过的那些岁月，终于一举追上了他。

en: An old woman who had no children was washing clothes by a river when a huge peach came floating down on the current. She carried it home, and when she and her husband cut it open, a baby boy was inside. They named him Momotaro, the Peach Boy, and raised him as their own son. He grew tall, strong, and bright, and the village loved him.

When Momotaro was old enough, he told his parents that he meant to sail to Onigashima, the island of the ogres who terrorized the country. His mother made him millet dumplings for the road. On his way, he met a dog, a monkey, and a pheasant, each of whom asked for one of his dumplings and joined him in return. Together the four set out for the ogres' island.

They crossed the sea and broke into the ogres' fortress. The dog bit, the monkey climbed and clawed, the pheasant struck from the air, and Momotaro fought hand to hand at their head. The ogre chief surrendered and gave up all his stolen treasures. Momotaro and his three companions loaded the treasure into a cart and returned home, where the old couple welcomed them with great joy.
zh: 一位无儿无女的老婆婆在河边洗衣，水面上漂来一颗硕大的桃子。她把桃子带回家中，与老爷爷一同剖开，竟从里面跳出一个男婴。他们给他取名「桃太郎」，把他当作亲生孩子抚养长大。桃太郎长成了一位英俊健壮、机敏过人的少年，深得乡里疼爱。
桃太郎长大后告诉老两口，他要远渡鬼岛，去讨伐那群常常滋扰乡间的鬼怪。老婆婆为他做好黍米团子带在路上。途中他先后遇到一只狗、一只猴和一只雉鸡，各以一只团子换得它们的同伴之情。四个一齐踏上了通往鬼岛的路。
他们渡海闯入鬼岛城寨。狗冲上去撕咬，猴攀高抓挠，雉鸡从空中啄击，桃太郎自己则当先与鬼怪短兵相接。鬼王终于屈膝求饶，把抢来的宝物悉数交出。桃太郎和三位同伴用车将宝物运回家乡，老两口欢欢喜喜地把他们迎了回来。

en: An old bamboo cutter went into the bamboo grove and saw that one stalk glowed with a strange light. Inside the stalk he found a tiny girl, no taller than his thumb. He brought her home and his wife raised her. From then on, every time the bamboo cutter found gold in the joints of the bamboo, and the household became wealthy. The child grew quickly into a young woman of dazzling beauty, and they called her Kaguya-hime, the Shining Princess.

Word of her beauty spread, and five great noblemen came in turn to ask for her hand. Kaguya-hime set each of them an impossible task: to bring her the begging bowl of the Buddha, a branch from a jeweled tree, a fire-rat's robe, a jewel from a dragon's neck, or a swallow's cowry. Each tried, and each failed or cheated, and she refused them all. Finally the emperor himself fell in love with her, but he too she would not marry.

As the years passed, Kaguya-hime grew sad whenever she looked up at the moon. At last she told the bamboo cutter that she was not of this world: she had come from the moon and must return there. On the night of the full moon, despite all the guards the emperor sent, a procession descended from the sky, dressed her in a feathered robe, and carried her up. As she put on the robe she forgot the earth, and her elderly parents were left looking up at the moon for her.
zh: 一位老竹翁走进竹林，看见有一根竹竿发出奇异的光。他在那根竹中找到一个只有拇指般大小的小女孩，把她带回了家，由老婆婆抚养。从此之后，竹翁每次砍竹时都能在竹节里找到金子，家境也渐渐殷实。那小女孩飞快长成了一位光彩照人的少女，人们便称她为「辉夜姬」，意为闪光的公主。
关于她的美名传开后，先后有五位大贵族前来求亲。辉夜姬给他们每人出了一道几乎不可能完成的难题：去取佛陀的钵、玉树的枝、火鼠的裘、龙颈的明珠或燕子的子安贝。五人或失败或行骗，没有一人能交出真物，她便一一回绝。最后连当朝天皇也爱上了她，她仍然不肯嫁。
岁月流逝，辉夜姬望见月亮时常常神色凄然。最终她向老竹翁坦言，她并非凡人，本是从月宫而来，如今必须回月去。月圆之夜，纵使天皇调来护卫层层守护，仍有一队天人从天而降，给她披上羽衣，把她接走。她披上羽衣的一瞬便忘了人间种种，老竹翁与老妪只能仰望那轮明月，留下她不再回望的背影。

en: After Susanoo was banished from the high plain of heaven, he came down to the land of Izumo. By the bank of a river he met an old couple weeping with their last daughter, the maiden Kushinada-hime. They told him that a giant serpent with eight heads and eight tails, Yamata-no-Orochi, came every year and devoured one of their daughters. Only this one was left, and the serpent's time was near.

Susanoo offered to save the girl in exchange for her hand. He told the old couple to brew strong sake and set it out in eight large vats behind a fence, with eight openings. When the serpent arrived, each of its eight heads dipped into one of the vats and drank. Soon all eight heads grew heavy with drink and lay down to sleep.

Susanoo drew his sword and hewed the sleeping serpent apart, piece by piece. Inside the tail he found a great sword shining among the cut flesh, which he took and offered to the sun goddess Amaterasu. The blade was named Kusanagi, and it became one of the three sacred treasures of Japan. Susanoo then married Kushinada-hime, and the people of Izumo were free of the serpent at last.
zh: 须佐之男被逐出高天原之后，降到了出云之地。他在河岸边遇到一对老夫妇正抱着最后一个女儿哭泣，那是少女櫛名田姫。他们告诉须佐之男，每年都有一条八头八尾的大蛇——八岐大蛇——前来吞食他们的女儿。如今只剩下这最后一个，大蛇又快要来了。
须佐之男答应救她，条件是娶她为妻。他让老夫妇酿好烈酒，分装在八只大酒桶里，再用围栏围起来，留出八个小口让大蛇伸头。大蛇到了之后，八颗头各探入一只酒桶喝个不停。不久八颗头都喝得沉醉，纷纷倒下沉沉入睡。
须佐之男拔剑而出，把熟睡的大蛇一段一段斩斫开来。劈到尾部时，他在断口的肉中看到一柄熠熠生辉的宝剑，便取出来献给天照大神。这柄剑后来被命名为草薙剑，成为日本三大神器之一。须佐之男随后与櫛名田姫成亲，出云的百姓从此再不必献女祭蛇。

en: The story of Dangun begins with the heavenly prince Hwanung, son of the lord of heaven Hwanin. Hwanung wished to live among human beings and asked his father's permission. Hwanin gave him three heavenly seals and sent him down to the world with three thousand followers. Hwanung descended on Mount Taebaek and founded a divine city, where he taught the people farming, medicine, justice, and the arts of life.

A bear and a tiger living near the mountain both prayed to Hwanung that they might become human. He gave each of them twenty cloves of garlic and a bundle of mugwort, telling them that if they could remain in a cave eating only those foods and not seeing sunlight for a hundred days, they would become human. The tiger could not bear it and gave up. The bear endured the trial and after only twenty-one days emerged as a woman.

The woman, called Ungnyeo, prayed for a child, and Hwanung married her. She gave birth to a son named Dangun. Dangun went on to found the first Korean kingdom, called Gojoseon, with his capital at Asadal. He is said to have ruled for over a thousand years before withdrawing into the mountains as a mountain god, and he became the legendary founder of the Korean people.
zh: 檀君的故事要从天上的桓雄说起。桓雄是天帝桓因之子，他希望下到人间生活，便求父亲准许。桓因赐他三件天符印，又派三千部众随他下凡。桓雄降在太白山，在那里建起神圣之邑，把农耕、医术、刑律和各种生活之道教给百姓。
山旁有一只熊和一只虎，都向桓雄祈求自己能化为人。桓雄给它们各一捆艾草和二十枚蒜瓣，告诉它们：只要在山洞里以这些为食、百日不见阳光，就能化为人形。老虎熬不住，半途放弃。熊老老实实坚持，仅二十一天就走出洞外，变成了一名女子。
这名女子被称为熊女，她祈求生子，桓雄便娶她为妻。她生下一个男孩，名叫檀君。檀君后来建立了朝鲜半岛上的第一个王国——古朝鲜，定都阿斯达。传说他在位一千多年，最后退入山中化为山神。后世便将他奉为朝鲜民族的始祖。

en: Rama was the eldest son of King Dasharatha of Ayodhya, and the rightful heir to the throne. On the eve of his coronation, his stepmother Kaikeyi reminded the king of two old boons he had once promised her. She asked that her own son Bharata be made king instead, and that Rama be sent into the forest for fourteen years. Dasharatha was heartbroken, but, bound by his word, he could not refuse.

Rama accepted the exile without protest, saying his father's word must be kept. His wife Sita and his younger brother Lakshmana insisted on following him into the forest. The three lived as ascetics in the wilderness, in simple huts, eating fruit and roots. From his palace, Bharata refused to take the throne; he kept Rama's sandals on the seat and ruled in his brother's name.

During the long exile the demon king Ravana, lord of Lanka, abducted Sita and carried her across the sea to his fortress. Rama, with Lakshmana, the monkey general Hanuman, and an army of allies, crossed the sea and made war on Lanka. He killed Ravana, rescued Sita, and returned at last to Ayodhya at the end of fourteen years. Bharata gave back the throne, and Rama was crowned in the city he had been forced to leave.
zh: 罗摩是阿逾陀城国王十车王的长子，本是王位正统继承人。就在他即位前夕，继母吉迦伊向国王讨回早年许下的两个恩赏。她要求让自己的儿子婆罗多继承王位，并把罗摩流放到森林里十四年。十车王心如刀绞，但因有诺在先，无法推辞。
罗摩毫无怨言地接受了这场流放，说父王的诺言不可违。妻子悉多和弟弟罗什曼那坚持要随他一同入林。三人在莽林中以隐士的方式过活，住简陋的草庐，以野果、根茎为食。婆罗多在宫中拒不登位，把罗摩的草鞋放在王座上，代兄治理国家。
流放的岁月里，魔王罗波那来到林中，把悉多劫走，渡海带回楞伽。罗摩与罗什曼那、神猴军师哈奴曼以及众多盟军一齐渡海，对楞伽展开大战。他斩杀罗波那，救回悉多，在十四年之期满时终于回到阿逾陀。婆罗多把王位归还于他，罗摩在那座他曾被迫离开的城里登上王位。

en: After Sita was carried off by Ravana to the island fortress of Lanka, Rama and his allies needed someone who could cross the wide ocean and find her. The monkey general Hanuman was chosen. Hanuman was a son of the wind god Vayu and possessed the gift of growing to any size at will. Standing on the shore he prepared himself with prayer, and the mountain beneath his feet seemed to push him upward as he leapt.

On the way across, the sea sent up a mountain to offer him rest, and a sea serpent and a demon both tried to stop him. He politely declined the mountain, slipped past the serpent, and shrank to enter the demon's mouth, then expanded suddenly and burst through to fly on. He landed in Lanka under the cover of night and made himself tiny so that he could move through the streets and palace without being seen.

In Ravana's garden he found Sita sitting alone among trees. He showed her Rama's signet ring so that she would know he was a true messenger and told her that Rama would come for her. Before leaving, he allowed himself to be caught by Ravana's son, his tail was set on fire as a punishment, and he leapt from roof to roof setting much of the city on fire. Then he leapt back over the sea to Rama with the news of where Sita was kept.
zh: 罗波那把悉多劫到楞伽岛之后，罗摩和盟军急需一人能横渡大海、查出她的下落。神猴军师哈奴曼自告奋勇。哈奴曼是风神伐由之子，能随意变大变小。他在岸边静静祈祷之后，脚下的山仿佛把他向上托起，他一跃而起，腾空跨海。
越海途中，海中升起一座山想请他歇脚，海蛇与一名罗刹也想拦下他。他婉拒山的好意，避开海蛇，又缩小身形钻入罗刹口中，再瞬间放大身躯破其口而出，继续飞向楞伽。夜色之下他降落楞伽，把自己变得极小，悄悄穿行在城中街巷和宫闱之间。
他在罗波那的园林中找到独坐林间的悉多。他出示罗摩的指环为信物，让她相信自己是真正的使者，并告诉她罗摩很快就会前来救她。临走前他故意被罗波那之子捉住，尾巴被点火示众，他便借机从屋顶到屋顶跳跃，把大半座楞伽都点着了火。然后他再次跃海而回，把悉多的下落禀报给罗摩。

en: Popocatepetl was a great warrior of the Aztec people, and Iztaccihuatl was the daughter of his ruler. The two of them loved each other, but her father would not consent to the marriage until Popocatepetl had proved himself by winning a war against a distant enemy. The warrior set out on the long campaign, and Iztaccihuatl waited for him in the city. A rival sent word back home that Popocatepetl had been killed in battle.

Iztaccihuatl, grief-stricken, sickened and died of her sorrow. When Popocatepetl returned victorious and found her dead, he refused to leave her side. He carried her body up a high mountain and laid her there, and he stood beside her with a smoking torch in his hand. There he meant to keep watch over her forever.

Over time the two of them became the two great snow-capped volcanoes that still stand near Mexico City. Iztaccihuatl's mountain, called the Sleeping Woman, has the outline of a woman lying in white snow. Popocatepetl's mountain stands beside hers, still smoking from the torch he holds. People of the central highlands have looked up at the two peaks for centuries as the figures of the lovers who could not be separated even by death.
zh: 波波卡特佩特是阿兹特克的一位勇士，伊斯塔西瓦特是他所属君主的女儿。两人相爱，但伊斯塔西瓦特的父亲不肯应允这门亲事，要波波卡特佩特先去打赢一场远方的战争来证明自己。勇士便启程出征，伊斯塔西瓦特留在城中等待。途中有个情敌假传消息，说波波卡特佩特已经战死。
伊斯塔西瓦特闻讯悲痛欲绝，重病不起，最终因哀伤而死。波波卡特佩特凯旋归来，发现她已逝去，无法离开她半步。他抱起她的身体登上一座高山，把她安放在山顶，自己手持一只点燃的火把守候在她身旁。他立誓要永远守护着她。
长年累月之后，二人化作了至今仍矗立在墨西哥城外的两座雪山。伊斯塔西瓦特的那座山，被称为「沉睡的女子」，山形宛如一位身披白雪安卧的女子。波波卡特佩特的山在她身旁，至今仍因他手中那把火炬而微微冒烟。中央高原一带的人们多少代以来仰望这一对山峰，把它们看作那对生死都未能分开的恋人。

en: The Philistines had brought out their champion Goliath, a giant nearly ten feet tall, who stood in the valley between the armies and challenged any Israelite to single combat. Day after day no Israelite dared to face him. The young shepherd David, sent by his father to bring food to his older brothers in the army, heard the giant's challenge. King Saul tried to give him armor, but David was not used to it and laid it aside.

David went down to the brook and chose five smooth stones for his sling. He came out to meet Goliath with only his staff and the sling in his hand. The giant looked at the boy with contempt and cursed him by his gods. David answered that he came in the name of the God of Israel, whose battle this was.

As Goliath advanced, David ran toward him, took a stone from his pouch, swung the sling, and struck the giant in the forehead. Goliath fell on his face, and David ran up, took the giant's own sword, and cut off his head. When the Philistines saw their champion dead, they fled, and the Israelite army pursued them. David's name spread through the kingdom from that day.
zh: 非利士人推出他们的勇士歌利亚，那是一名身高近三米的巨人，每天在两军之间的山谷中挑战，要以色列军中派人与他一对一决斗。日复一日，以色列军中无人敢出阵迎战。年轻的牧羊人大卫奉父亲之命，给在军中的几位兄长送干粮，在阵前听到了巨人的叫嚣。扫罗王想把铠甲让给大卫穿，大卫穿不惯，便又脱了下来。
大卫走到溪边，挑了五块光滑的石子放进自己的牧囊。他手里只拿着牧杖和一具甩石索，便走出去迎战歌利亚。巨人轻蔑地看着这个少年，按自己的神咒诅他。大卫回答说，他是奉以色列神的名而来，这场仗本就是神的争战。
歌利亚向前迈步时，大卫朝他奔去，从囊中取出一块石子，挥动甩石索掷出，正中巨人的额头。歌利亚扑倒在地，大卫赶上前，抽出巨人自己的刀，把他的头砍了下来。非利士人见自家勇士已死，纷纷溃逃，以色列大军在后追击。从此大卫的名字传遍全国。

en: The prophet Jonah was told by God to go to Nineveh, a great and wicked city, and warn its people to repent. Jonah refused the task and instead went down to the port of Joppa, paid his fare, and boarded a ship sailing in the opposite direction. While he was at sea a violent storm rose up, and the ship seemed about to break apart. The sailors cast lots to find out whose fault it was, and the lot fell on Jonah.

He told them that he was fleeing from his God and that they should throw him into the sea to save themselves. After trying without success to row to land, the sailors finally cast him overboard. The storm grew calm at once. In the deep water a great fish swallowed Jonah whole, and he stayed alive inside it for three days and three nights.

From inside the fish Jonah prayed, and God commanded the fish to vomit him out onto dry land. Jonah went on to Nineveh and delivered the warning. The whole city, from the king down, repented in sackcloth and ashes, and God spared them. Jonah was angry that the city had not been destroyed, but God reproached him for caring more about his own comfort than about the lives of so many people.
zh: 先知约拿奉神之命前往尼尼微，一座庞大而充满恶行的城，宣告那里的人若不悔改将受惩罚。约拿不愿担此差事，反而下到约帕海港，付了船费，登上一艘开往相反方向的船。海上忽起狂风大浪，船几乎要被掀翻。水手们抽签查问灾祸的原由，签子落在约拿身上。
约拿向他们承认自己是在逃避耶和华，要他们把他抛进海里以求平安。水手们先是奋力划桨想靠岸，无奈风浪太大，最后只好将他丢入海中。瞬间风浪止息。海中有一条大鱼把约拿整个吞下去，他在鱼腹里活着待了三天三夜。
在鱼腹中约拿向神祷告，神命大鱼把他吐到陆地上。约拿这才前往尼尼微，传达神的警告。全城上下从君王到百姓都披麻蒙灰悔改，神便收回原本要降下的灾祸。约拿因尼尼微未被毁灭而怒，神却责问他：为何只在意自己舒坦与否，却不顾这许多人的死活。

en: Samson was a Hebrew judge with extraordinary strength, dedicated to God from birth on the condition that his hair was never cut. He fought the Philistines almost alone, tearing apart a lion with his bare hands and once striking down a thousand men with the jawbone of an ass. The Philistines learned that they could not defeat him in open battle. They turned instead to a woman named Delilah, and offered her silver to find out the secret of his strength.

Delilah pressed Samson again and again to tell her the source of his power. Three times he gave her false answers; each time she tested them while the Philistines waited in hiding, and each time he broke free. At last he told her the truth: if his hair were cut, his strength would leave him. While he slept on her knees, she had a man cut off the seven locks of his hair.

The Philistines seized him, blinded him, and bound him to grind grain in a prison. Time passed, and his hair began to grow back. At a great festival the Philistine lords called him out as a spectacle, between the two pillars of their temple. Samson asked a boy to lead him to the pillars, leaned against them, prayed for strength one last time, and pushed. The temple collapsed, killing him and more enemies in his death than in his life.
zh: 参孙是希伯来人的士师，天生力大无穷，从出生起就将归神，不可剃头。他几乎是独自对付非利士人，曾徒手撕开一头狮子，又用一根驴腮骨击杀千名敌兵。非利士人发现自己在正面对抗中根本胜不过他。于是他们去找一名女子大利拉，许她重金，让她设法探出参孙力气的秘密。
大利拉一次次缠住参孙，问他的力量从何而来。参孙三次以假话敷衍她，每一次大利拉都按所说试他，非利士人埋伏在旁等着，但他都轻易挣脱。最后他终于把实情告诉她：若头发被剃，他的力气便会消失。大利拉趁他枕在她膝上睡熟，让人把他七绺头发剃尽。
非利士人随即将他擒住、剜去双目，把他绑在监狱里推磨。日子一天天过去，他的头发渐渐长出来。某次盛大节庆，非利士首领把他押到神庙作为众人取笑的对象，叫他立在两根大柱之间。参孙叫领路的孩童把他扶到柱旁，倚靠两柱向神最后求力，用力一推。整座神庙轰然倒塌，他一死所杀的敌人，比他一生杀的还多。

en: Two women came before King Solomon, both claiming that the same newborn baby was theirs. They lived in the same house and had each given birth at around the same time. One of the babies had died in the night, and each woman accused the other of switching the bodies. There were no witnesses, and each told the same story from her own side.

Solomon listened to both of them and then called for a sword. He ordered that the living child be cut in two, so that each woman could have half. The first woman, weeping, begged the king to spare the baby and give it to the other rather than kill it. The second woman agreed coldly that the child should be divided.

Solomon at once gave the baby to the first woman, declaring that she was its true mother, because only a mother would rather lose her child than see it dead. When the people of Israel heard about the judgment, they were filled with awe and recognized that the wisdom of God was upon the young king. The case became a famous test of how a true heart could be told from a false one.
zh: 两个妇人来到所罗门王面前，都说同一个刚出生的婴儿是自己的。她们住在同一屋檐下，几乎在同一时间各生了一个孩子。一个孩子半夜里死了，两人都指控对方调换了死活婴儿。又没有旁人作证，两人各执一词。
所罗门听完两人的陈述，便命人取剑来。他下令把那活着的孩子劈成两半，让两个妇人各得一半。其中一个妇人含泪苦求国王，宁可把孩子整个给另一个妇人，也不要看着他被劈。另一个妇人却冷冷地附和，说就该把孩子分了。
所罗门当即把婴儿判给前一个妇人，宣布她才是真正的母亲，因为只有真正的母亲，宁可失去孩子也不忍见他被害。以色列百姓听到这桩判决无不敬畏，明白这位年轻的国王身上有神的智慧。此案也成了后世辨别真情与假意的著名典故。

en: A couple longed for a child, and when the wife finally became pregnant she began to crave a herb called rapunzel from a witch's garden next door. The husband climbed the wall night after night to steal it, until the witch caught him. She agreed to let him go on the condition that they give her the child when it was born. When the baby girl was born, the witch took her away and named her Rapunzel.

Rapunzel grew up shut in a tall tower without doors or stairs. She had very long, beautiful golden hair. When the witch wanted to come up, she called, "Rapunzel, Rapunzel, let down your hair," and used the hair as a ladder. One day a prince passing through the woods heard Rapunzel singing and watched the witch's ascent. After the witch had gone, he called the same words, climbed up, and met Rapunzel.

The prince and Rapunzel agreed to marry, but the witch found out, cut off her hair, and abandoned her in a wilderness. When the prince came again that night, the witch let down the cut hair to trick him, and as he climbed up she dropped him into a thorn bush below, which blinded him. He wandered the wilderness for years until he heard Rapunzel's voice again. Her tears fell on his eyes and his sight was restored, and they returned to his kingdom together.
zh: 一对夫妇早就盼着有个孩子，妻子终于怀孕之后，开始一心想吃隔壁巫婆园中的一种菜——长发菜（拉庞采尔）。丈夫一连几夜翻墙进去偷菜，最后被巫婆抓个正着。巫婆答应饶他，但条件是孩子生下来要交给她。女婴出生后果然被巫婆抱走，并取名拉庞采尔。
拉庞采尔被关在一座既无门又无梯的高塔里长大。她有一头非常长而美丽的金发。巫婆每次要上塔，就在塔下喊：「拉庞采尔，拉庞采尔，把你的头发放下来。」然后顺着她的长发往上爬。有一天，一位王子路过林中，听见塔上传来歌声，又见巫婆爬上去。等巫婆走后，他用同样的话喊上去，爬上塔，结识了拉庞采尔。
王子与拉庞采尔约定结为夫妻，巫婆察觉之后，怒剪她的长发，又把她抛在荒野中。当晚王子再来塔下时，巫婆放下那截剪下的头发骗他爬上去，等他爬到一半便松手，他坠入下方的荆棘丛中，双眼被刺瞎。他在荒野里漂泊多年，直到再次听到拉庞采尔的歌声。她的泪水落在他眼睛上，他重又看见，两人这才一同回到他的国土。

en: Hansel and Gretel were the children of a poor woodcutter. In a time of hunger their stepmother persuaded their father to lead them deep into the forest and leave them there. Hansel overheard the plan and filled his pockets with white pebbles; on the way he dropped them one by one, and that night the moon lit them up so the children walked home along the trail. A second time the stepmother insisted, and Hansel could only drop crumbs of bread, which the birds ate.

Lost in the woods, the children at last came to a cottage made of bread, cake, and sugar candy. They began to nibble it, and an old woman invited them inside and fed them well. She was in fact a witch who ate children. She locked Hansel in a cage to fatten him up, and made Gretel serve as her maid. Each day the witch felt Hansel's finger to see if he was plump enough, and each day he held out a thin bone instead.

When the witch lost patience and ordered Gretel to climb into the hot oven to see if it was ready, Gretel pretended she did not understand. As the witch leaned in to show her, Gretel pushed her into the oven and shut the door. The children freed each other, took the witch's jewels from the cottage, and found their way home. There they learned that their stepmother had died, and they lived with their father in better times after.
zh: 汉塞尔和格蕾特是穷樵夫的一双儿女。家里闹饥荒，继母劝丈夫把孩子带进森林深处丢下。汉塞尔偷听到这个计划，悄悄在衣袋里装满白卵石；走在路上他一颗一颗地丢下，当晚月光照得石子闪亮，兄妹俩便顺着石子的踪迹回了家。继母又一次坚持，汉塞尔这回只来得及带面包，把面包屑丢在路上，结果被小鸟吃光了。
兄妹俩迷路在林中，最后看到一座用面包、糕点和糖果做成的小屋。他们刚开始啃，屋里出来一个老妇人，把他们让进屋里好生招待。其实那是一个专吃孩子的女巫。她把汉塞尔关进笼子里养胖，又把格蕾特留下来做使女。每天女巫都伸手摸汉塞尔的手指看胖了没有，他每天都伸出一根细骨头蒙混过去。
女巫等得失去耐心，命格蕾特爬进烧热的炉子里看烤好了没。格蕾特假装听不懂。女巫俯身探头要做示范时，格蕾特猛地把她推进炉里，关上了炉门。兄妹相互打开锁链，把女巫屋里的珠宝拿走，找到回家的路。回到家发现继母已死，从此与父亲过上了较为安宁的日子。

en: A miller, eager to impress the king, boasted that his daughter could spin straw into gold. The king shut her in a room piled with straw and told her to spin it all by morning, or she would die. As she wept, a small strange man appeared in the room and offered to do the spinning in exchange for her necklace. He spun all the straw into gold by morning, and the king was amazed.

The next night the king set her a larger room of straw and the same threat. The little man came again and spun gold in exchange for her ring. On the third night, with an even larger room, she had nothing left to give. The little man said she could pay him with her first-born child once she became queen. She agreed because she had no other choice.

The king married her, and after the queen had her first child the little man returned to claim it. Seeing her despair, he gave her three days to guess his name; if she could name him, she could keep the child. On the third night her messenger overheard him chanting his name by a fire in the woods — Rumpelstiltskin. When the queen spoke the name back to him, he flew into such a rage that he tore himself in two.
zh: 一位磨坊主想在国王面前夸耀，便吹嘘自己的女儿能把稻草纺成黄金。国王便把她关进一间堆满稻草的屋子，命她一夜之间纺尽，否则就处死她。她正哭得绝望时，屋里突然出现一个矮小奇怪的小人，提出愿用她的项链交换替她纺。他果然一夜把所有稻草纺成黄金，国王大为惊叹。
第二夜国王给她换了一间更大的屋子，要求和威胁照旧。那小人又来了，这次换走了她的戒指替她纺金。到了第三夜，屋子更大，她身上已无物可与之交换。小人说，等她做了王后，可以用第一个孩子来抵账。她别无选择，便答应了。
国王果然娶她为后，等她生下第一个孩子之后，小人前来索讨。看见她痛苦不堪，他给她三天时间，让她去猜他的名字；若猜对，便不取走孩子。第三晚她派去的信差在林中火堆旁偷听到他自唱其名——侏儒怪（Rumpelstiltskin）。王后把这名字当面说出后，小人勃然大怒，竟把自己活活撕成了两半。

en: The town of Hamelin was overrun with rats, so many and so bold that nothing could be done with them. A stranger arrived dressed in colored clothes, carrying a flute, and offered to rid the town of every rat in exchange for a sum of money. The townspeople agreed. He began to play his flute, and rats came out from every house, from kitchens and cellars and barns, and followed him in a great moving carpet.

He led them out of the town to the river Weser, where they all drowned. When he returned to collect his payment, the townspeople refused him or paid him less than the promised sum. The piper said nothing in answer; he turned and walked away. Some days later, while the adults of Hamelin were in the church, he returned and played a different tune in the streets.

This time the children of the town came running out of every house and followed him. They walked in a long line behind him out of the town and toward a hillside, where, the old stories say, the hillside opened and closed behind them. Only one or two children, who could not keep up, came back; the rest were never seen again. The townspeople had to live with the silence of empty rooms and the memory of the bargain they had broken.
zh: 哈默林城里鼠患横行，鼠群多到无法可治。一个穿着彩衣、手持笛子的陌生人来到城里，提出只要给他一笔报酬，他能把全城的老鼠都清理干净。市民们答应了。他吹起笛子，老鼠从家家户户、厨房、地窖、谷仓里涌出来，跟在他身后排成一条流动的长河。
他领着鼠群走出城外，到了威悉河边，鼠群一只接一只地淹死在水中。等他回去领赏，市民们却拒绝付他，或是只肯付一小部分。吹笛人没有争辩，转身就走了。过了几天，正当大人们在教堂礼拜时，他又回到城里，街道上响起一支与上次不同的曲子。
这一次城里的孩子从家家户户跑出来，跟在他身后走出城外，去到一座山坡。古老传说说，那山坡裂开又合上，孩子们从此再也没有出来。只有一两个跟不上的孩子留下，其余全部失踪。城里只剩下空空的房间和那次毁约之事留下的回声。

en: In old Switzerland, a foreign bailiff named Gessler ruled over the town of Altdorf with great cruelty. He set up his hat on a pole in the marketplace and ordered every passerby to bow to it. The expert crossbowman William Tell walked past with his young son and refused to bow. Gessler arrested him on the spot.

As a punishment, Gessler ordered Tell to place an apple on his son's head and shoot it off with a single bolt of his crossbow. If he missed or refused, both he and the boy would die. Tell drew a second bolt before he shot and hid it in his coat. With his first shot he struck the apple cleanly from his son's head.

Gessler asked him why he had hidden the second bolt. Tell answered that, if the first shot had killed his son, the second was meant for Gessler himself. The bailiff had Tell bound and taken away by boat, but Tell escaped during a storm on the lake, lay in wait in a narrow pass, and shot Gessler dead. His act became a signal for the Swiss to rise against their oppressors.
zh: 古时瑞士有一位异乡来的太守名叫盖斯勒，他在阿尔特多夫横行霸道。他把自己的帽子挂在市集的木杆上，命令每一个过路人都要朝帽子鞠躬。神射手威廉·退尔带着小儿子从下面走过，偏不行礼。盖斯勒当场将他扣下。
作为惩罚，盖斯勒命退尔把一只苹果放在儿子头上，用十字弓一箭射下来。若射偏或拒射，父子二人都要被处死。射箭之前，退尔从弓囊里又抽出一支箭暗藏在怀里。他举弩第一箭就把苹果干净利落地从儿子头上射落。
盖斯勒问他怀里那第二支箭是做什么用的。退尔答道：若第一箭误中了儿子，第二箭就是留给他自己用的。太守听罢，下令把退尔捆起来用船押走，途中湖上起了大风暴，退尔趁乱脱身，在一条山口设伏，一箭射死了盖斯勒。这一箭也成了瑞士百姓反抗压迫者的信号。

en: Robin Hood was an outlawed yeoman who lived in Sherwood Forest with a band of merry men. He had been driven into hiding by the corrupt local lords during the absence of King Richard from England. From the cover of the great wood he and his men preyed on the rich travelers who passed through and used the gold they took to help the poor. The Sheriff of Nottingham, who took bribes from the lords, tried again and again to capture him.

Among Robin's company were Little John, the giant who beat him with a quarterstaff at their first meeting and then joined his band; Friar Tuck, the bold and hungry monk; Will Scarlet; and Maid Marian, the woman he loved. Their fame spread through the countryside as they outwitted the sheriff at every turn. Once, in disguise, Robin even entered an archery contest set by the sheriff and won the golden arrow as the prize before slipping away.

When King Richard at last returned from the crusades, he came in disguise into Sherwood to meet the outlaws himself. Robin and his men entertained the stranger, then learned with joy that he was the king. Richard pardoned them and took some of the band back to court with him. The Sheriff of Nottingham lost his power, and Sherwood Forest was remembered ever after as the home of a band of outlaws who had stood for the poor against the great.
zh: 罗宾汉本是一个被逐出户籍的自由民，住在舍伍德森林里，身边聚着一群快乐伙伴。他是趁着国王理查远征不在国内时，被腐败的地方贵族害得不得不躲进林子里的。他和伙伴们就在大片森林中拦截路过的富商富贵，把劫得的金银分给穷人。诺丁汉郡治安官从权贵那里收受好处，几度想抓他，都未能得手。
罗宾的同伙中有：与他初识时用木棍把他打下桥、然后加入他的巨人小约翰；胆大食量也大的修士塔克；威尔·斯卡莱特；以及他心爱的玛丽安姑娘。他们一次次让治安官落空，名声传遍乡野。某次治安官摆下射箭比赛，罗宾甚至乔装混进去夺得金箭头大奖，然后从容溜走。
等到狮心王理查终于结束十字军东征回国，他乔装来到舍伍德，想亲自见一见这群绿林好汉。罗宾和伙伴们款待这位陌生客人，得知来人正是国王后大喜过望。理查赦免了他们，将其中一些人随自己带回宫廷效力。诺丁汉治安官从此失势，舍伍德森林也因此成了一个永远被记住的地方——那里曾住着一群替穷人和权贵作对的绿林好汉。

en: Jack lived alone with his widowed mother. They were so poor that one day his mother told him to take their last cow to market and sell it. On the road he met a stranger who offered him a handful of beans in exchange for the cow, promising that they were magic beans. Jack made the trade and brought the beans home. His mother, furious, threw them out of the window.

By the next morning a huge beanstalk had grown up to the clouds. Jack climbed it and at the top found a great castle that belonged to a giant. He slipped inside while the giant's wife was distracted and hid. He saw the giant come home, smell that he had a stranger in the house, and then settle to count his bags of gold and at last to sleep. Jack took a bag of gold and climbed back down.

He climbed up again on later days and took a hen that laid golden eggs, and then a magic harp that played by itself. The harp cried out to its master, and the giant chased Jack down the beanstalk. Jack reached the bottom first and called for an axe. He cut through the beanstalk; the giant fell from the sky and was killed, and Jack and his mother were rich for the rest of their days.
zh: 杰克和守寡的母亲相依为命，家境贫寒。一天母亲叫他把家里仅剩的一头牛牵到集市卖掉。半路他遇到一个陌生人，提出用一把豆子换走那头牛，并声称这些是魔豆。杰克便换了下来带回家。母亲气坏了，把豆子从窗口扔到院里。
第二天一早，一棵巨大的豆茎已经直冲云霄。杰克沿着豆茎爬到天上，看到云端有一座属于巨人的大城堡。趁巨人的妻子不留意时他溜进屋里藏好。他看到巨人回家闻到生人气，先点数一袋袋金币，再渐渐沉沉睡去。杰克偷偷拿下一袋金子，顺着豆茎爬回家。
之后他又爬上去几回，先拿走一只会下金蛋的母鸡，又拿走一架会自动弹奏的魔琴。魔琴向主人呼救，巨人沿着豆茎追了下来。杰克抢先到达地面，喊母亲取斧。他把豆茎砍断，巨人从空中坠落摔死。从此杰克与母亲衣食无忧，安享余生。

en: A king and queen long childless were at last granted a daughter. They held a great christening and invited the fairies of the kingdom, but they had one too few golden plates, and one fairy was overlooked. As the fairies gave their gifts of beauty, virtue, and grace, the slighted fairy appeared and pronounced a curse: on her fifteenth birthday, the princess would prick herself on a spindle and die. The last fairy, who had not yet spoken, softened the curse: the princess would not die, but sleep for a hundred years.

The king ordered every spindle in the kingdom destroyed. But on her fifteenth birthday, the princess wandered into an old tower of the palace where one woman was still spinning. She tried the spindle, pricked her finger, and fell into a deep sleep. The same sleep fell on the entire palace — king, queen, servants, animals, fire on the hearth — and a forest of thorns grew up around the walls so that no one could pass through.

After a hundred years a prince from another country heard of the sleeping castle and rode out to find it. The thorns parted to let him pass. He went through the silent halls and found the princess on her bed, and kissed her. She opened her eyes, and at the same moment the whole castle woke as if no time had passed at all. The two were married, and the kingdom returned to its life.
zh: 国王和王后多年无子，终于盼来一个女儿。他们办了一场盛大的命名礼，邀请国中众仙女前来，但宫中所备的金盘少了一只，有一位仙女被遗漏在外。仙女们依次祝赐美貌、品德与仪态时，那位未受邀的仙女忽然出现，下了诅咒：公主在十五岁生日那天会被纺锤刺到而死。最后一位还未开口的仙女把诅咒减轻了一些：公主不会死，而是沉睡一百年。
国王下令把全国的纺锤都毁掉。可十五岁生日那天，公主信步走进宫中一座旧塔，塔里还有一位老妇人正在纺纱。她伸手去试那纺锤，手指被扎，应声沉沉睡去。同样的睡意笼罩了整座宫殿——国王、王后、仆役、动物、灶上的火都一齐睡去，宫墙外则长出茂密的荆棘林，无人能进入。
一百年后，一位来自远方的王子听说了这座沉睡的城堡，便驱马前来。荆棘自动分开放他通过。他穿过寂静的厅堂，找到躺在床上的公主，吻了她一下。公主睁开眼睛的瞬间，整座城堡也像不曾过过一秒钟般醒来。两人成婚，王国又恢复了往日的生气。

en: A wealthy merchant lost his fortune and moved with his children into a small house in the country. His youngest daughter, called Beauty, did not complain. One winter the merchant heard that one of his ships might still be saved; before leaving on the journey he asked the children what gifts they wished for. His other daughters asked for fine clothes; Beauty asked only for a rose. On the way back his road took him through a strange wood, and he was caught by night near a great empty palace.

The doors of the palace opened by themselves, food and fire awaited him, and in the morning he saw a garden full of roses. He picked one for Beauty, and at once a hideous Beast appeared and roared that he had been about to forgive him until that theft. The Beast spared him only on the condition that he send one of his daughters to live with him. Beauty insisted on going in her father's place.

At the palace, Beauty found that the Beast treated her with great gentleness, although his form frightened her. Each evening he asked her to marry him, and each evening she refused. When she returned briefly to her father's house and stayed longer than promised, she had a dream that the Beast was dying of grief. She rushed back, found him near death in the garden, and confessed that she loved him. The spell broke; the Beast became a handsome prince, and they were married.
zh: 一位富商家道中落，带着儿女搬到乡间的一座小屋里安身。他最小的女儿被人称作「美女」，从不抱怨。某年冬天商人得知自己一艘货船或许还能挽回，便准备启程去看看。临行前他问儿女想要什么礼物，其他女儿要华服首饰，只有美女说想要一枝玫瑰。归途中他走进一片陌生的森林，天黑时来到了一座空无一人的大宫殿。
宫门自行打开，桌上摆着饭菜，炉里燃着火。第二天早上，他在花园中看到满园玫瑰，便摘了一枝想带给美女。一个面目狰狞的「野兽」立刻现身，怒吼道：他正打算原谅商人的擅入，却被这一摘玫瑰所毁。野兽提出条件：除非商人送一个女儿来代他居住，否则就要处死他。美女坚持要替父亲前往。
美女在宫中住下后，发现野兽虽相貌可怖，对她却极为温柔。每天傍晚，野兽都向她求婚，她每天都温言回绝。有一次她回家探父，逾期未归，梦中却见野兽因忧伤而濒死。她急忙赶回宫中，在花园里见他奄奄一息，便对他坦白说自己爱他。咒语破解，野兽化作一位俊美的王子，二人成婚。

en: Rip Van Winkle was a kindly, easygoing villager in the Catskill mountains, more fond of helping neighbors than of working on his own farm. His wife scolded him constantly for his laziness. To escape her tongue he often took his gun and his dog into the mountains. One day he climbed higher than usual and met a small, oddly dressed man carrying a keg uphill.

The man led him to a hollow where strange bearded figures were playing at ninepins in complete silence. Rip helped carry the keg and was given some of its strange liquor to drink. Soon he felt very sleepy and lay down. When he woke up, his beard had grown long, his gun had rusted apart, and his dog was gone. He walked back down toward his village in confusion.

Nobody in the village recognized him. The signs over the inn had changed, the king's portrait had been replaced with a stranger called George Washington, and the world spoke of a recent war he had not heard of. Twenty years had passed while he slept in the mountains. At last he found a grown-up daughter who took him in, and he settled into the village again, telling his story to anyone who would listen.
zh: 李伯·范·温克尔是卡茨基尔山下村中一个性情温厚、好吃懒做的村民，宁可替邻居跑腿也不愿打理自家的田地。他妻子整日为此责骂他。为躲清静，他常带着猎枪和狗到山里去。一天他比平日爬得更高，遇到一个矮小、衣着古怪的人，正吃力地背着一只酒桶上山。
那人把他带到一处山坳，几个长须老头一声不吭地在那里玩九柱戏。李伯帮忙抬桶，得到一口桶里奇怪的酒喝下。不一会儿他便困倦得不行，倒头睡去。醒来时，他的胡子长得老长，猎枪已经锈烂，狗也不见踪影。他懵懵懂懂地下山走回村里。
村里却没人认得他了。客栈门上的招牌变了，墙上挂的国王肖像换成了一位叫华盛顿的陌生人，人人都谈论着一场他从没听说过的战争。他在山中一觉，竟过去了二十年。最后他找到一个已经长大的女儿，把他接回家中。从此他在村里安顿下来，每个肯听他的人，都听他讲那段往事。

en: The valley known as Sleepy Hollow, near the old Dutch town of Tarrytown, was famous for its strange tales. The most fearsome of all was the Headless Horseman, said to be the ghost of a Hessian trooper whose head had been carried off by a cannonball during the Revolutionary War. He was said to ride at night in search of his lost head. The schoolmaster Ichabod Crane came to teach in the valley and lodged with the local farmers.

Tall, awkward, and superstitious, Ichabod fell in love with Katrina van Tassel, the only daughter of a wealthy farmer. He was not the only suitor; the strong, mischievous Brom Bones was also after her. After a harvest party at the van Tassel farm, where the old stories of the Hollow had been told one after the other, Ichabod rode home alone through the dark woods on a borrowed horse.

On the way he heard hoofbeats behind him and saw, faintly outlined against the night, a dark horseman with no head, carrying a pumpkin under one arm. Ichabod galloped for the bridge that the spirit was said not to cross. As he reached it, the horseman threw the pumpkin at his head. The next morning the horse came back without him, and only a shattered pumpkin was found on the road. Brom Bones married Katrina, and the village argued about whether Ichabod had fled in shame or been carried off by the Horseman.
zh: 沉睡谷紧邻荷裔小镇塔里镇，以怪谈众多而闻名。最让人闻之色变的是「无头骑士」，据说是独立战争时一名头被炮弹打飞的黑森雇佣兵的鬼魂。人人都说他夜里出没，到处寻找失去的头颅。年轻的塾师伊卡博·克兰来到山谷教书，借住在当地农户家中。
伊卡博又高又瘦、举止笨拙、又特别迷信。他爱上了富户之女卡特琳娜·范·塔塞尔，可她身边并非只他一个追求者，强壮顽皮的布罗姆·邦斯也在追她。范家秋收聚会上，乡人轮流讲过沉睡谷的种种怪谈之后，伊卡博便骑着借来的马，一人摸黑穿过林子回家。
半路上他听到身后传来马蹄声，借着夜色看见一名黑乎乎的骑士追在身后，没有头颅，胳膊下夹着一只南瓜。伊卡博拼命驱马朝那座据说鬼怪过不去的小桥奔去。刚到桥头，那骑士一抬手把南瓜朝他头上掷过来。次日清晨，那匹马独自回到主人家，路上只见一只摔碎的南瓜。布罗姆·邦斯娶了卡特琳娜，村里人则一直议论：伊卡博究竟是羞愧潜逃，还是被那无头骑士带走了。

en: Paul Bunyan was a giant lumberjack of the North Woods, so big that as a baby he had been rocked in a cradle floated out on the sea. By the time he was a man he could carry a logger's axe the size of a tree. He took a young blue ox named Babe as his partner, and Babe grew to be almost as large as he was. Together they roamed the great forests of the United States and Canada.

Wherever they went, the two of them did the work of an entire camp. Paul's footprints made lakes. The Mississippi River, the old stories say, was formed when Babe dragged a leaky water tank across the plains. The Grand Canyon was hewn out when Paul once dragged his axe behind him. There was no woodlot too big for them to clear in a single season.

Paul ran great logging camps with rows of bunkhouses miles long, fed his men flapjacks the size of wagon wheels, and could read the weather from how Babe's tail hung. Tales of his strength and Babe's spread from camp to camp across the timber country, growing taller in every telling. When the great pine forests of the Midwest were finally felled and the lumber trade moved on, Paul and Babe were said to have walked north, looking for woods still wide enough for them to work in.
zh: 保罗·班扬是北方林海里一位巨人般的伐木工，从小就大得离谱，婴儿时甚至要躺在大海上飘着的摇篮里。长大成人时，他扛的伐木斧头大得像一棵树。后来他收了一头叫贝比的蓝牛做伙伴，贝比长大后几乎和他一般大。两个一起在美国和加拿大的莽莽森林里到处劳作。
他们走到哪里就把哪里整片林子伐完。保罗的脚印踩出来一座座湖泊。古老传说说，密西西比河就是贝比拖着一只漏水的水箱穿过平原时形成的。大峡谷则是保罗有一次拖着斧头走过留下的沟壑。再大的林场，他们一季就能伐完。
保罗的伐木营连绵几英里，给伐木工人做的薄煎饼大得像车轮一般。他能从贝比尾巴的姿势看出天气好坏。他和贝比的故事在大大小小的伐木营里口耳相传，越传越夸张。等中西部的松林差不多被砍光、伐木业向别处转移时，人们都说保罗和贝比往北走了，要去找一片仍然容得下他们干活的大森林。

en: John Henry was an African American railroad worker on the Chesapeake and Ohio Railroad in the years after the Civil War. He was a "steel-driver," a man who hammered long steel drills into rock to prepare it for blasting through mountain tunnels. He was famous for his strength and for the songs the men sang as he worked. The crews drove the road through the Big Bend Mountain in West Virginia in the 1870s.

The company brought in a new steam-powered drill that could cut into rock faster than any man. The bosses said the steam drill would soon replace human steel-drivers altogether. John Henry stepped up and said he would race the machine, hammer against piston. The two were set up side by side on the rock face, and the contest began.

For hours John Henry swung two heavy hammers, one in each hand, while the steam drill chewed forward on the other side. When the dust settled, his hole was deeper, and he had outdrilled the machine. But the strain had been too much. He lay down on the rock with his hammer beside him and died there. The song of John Henry and his hammer spread through every railroad and labor camp in America, as a memory of a man who had beaten the machine but paid for it with his life.
zh: 约翰·亨利是南北战争后切萨皮克与俄亥俄铁路上的一名非裔美国工人。他是「钢钎工」，专门用大锤把长长的钢钎砸进山石里，为打通山中隧道做爆破准备。他以力大无穷出名，伐石的节奏也成了工友们干活时唱的号子。1870 年代，他们正在西弗吉尼亚的大弯山中开凿隧道。
那段时间，公司新购进一种蒸汽钻，比任何工人砸钢钎都快。工头扬言这种机器很快会把人力钢钎工取而代之。约翰·亨利挺身而出，说他要与机器比一场，用自己的大锤对它的蒸汽活塞。两者在岩面上并排架好，比赛开始。
约翰·亨利一连数小时左右开弓，双手各执一柄重锤，机器在另一侧也咬着岩石不停往里钻。等尘烟落定，他那一侧的孔比机器深，硬是把蒸汽钻压在了下风。可这一战已用尽他全部气力。他在岩面上躺下，大锤倒在身旁，就此咽气。从那以后，关于约翰·亨利和他那柄大锤的歌谣，传遍美国所有的铁路工地和劳工营，提醒着人们：曾有一个人战胜了机器，但是用自己的性命换来的。

en: Alonso Quijano was an aging country gentleman of La Mancha who had read so many books of chivalry that he came to believe he was himself a knight-errant. He polished an old suit of armor, named his thin horse Rocinante, and gave himself the name Don Quixote. He persuaded a local farmer, Sancho Panza, to come with him as his squire, promising him an island to govern if their adventures went well.

Riding across the plains of La Mancha, Don Quixote saw thirty or forty windmills in the distance. He told Sancho that they were enormous giants, and that fighting them would be a glorious deed.
